The examples are available on our AI-related resources webpage and our patent eligibility page on our website. Patent searches require you to search different databases or specialized libraries to see whether there are any identical, similar, or partly similar inventions to your inventi\\\\n\\\\nPatents are property rights granted to an inventor in order to exclude others from making, using, selling, or importing a particular invention. If you are applying for a patent, a patent search is not legally required, but it can be a crucial part of figuring out whether any prior patent will interfere with your ability to acquire a patent. A patent search also helps you avoid patent infringement, write your patent application, understand the competition, and learn more about the field in which your invention belongs. Filing for a patent and acquiring a patent can be expensive. Before applying for a patent, you should conduct a search for \\\\u201cprior art,\\\\u201d which can save you money in the long run. Prior art is any existing body of knowledge relating to your invention, such as previous patents, publications, trade journal articles, public discussions, or sales anywhere in the world.\\\",\\n1636:        \\\"text\\\": \\\"A patent examiner will examine the prior art and look at all previous patents for the same or highly similar inventions. When all the features of your invention are found in a single earlier patent, the patent will be rejected for lacking novelty. In order to make sure an invention is novel, inventors should conduct a patent search before filing. The purpose of this requirement is to stop prior art from being patented again. ... The law regarding the patentability of inventions can be found in 35 U.S.C. Chapter 10. The patent law specifies that the subject matter must be \\\\u201cuseful.\\\\u201d This traditionally meant three things: practical utility, operability, and beneficial utility. However, the question of whether something has a beneficial use, something that is considered not immoral or deceptive, has not recently barred applications. Generally, a process, machine, or composition must operate to perform an intended purpose in the real world to meet this requirement.\\\\n\\\\nChoosing Among Patent, Copyright, and Trademark for Legal Protection \\\\u00b7 Intellectual Property Law FAQs \\\\u00b7 Find an Intellectual Property Lawyer \\\\u00b7 Related Areas \\\\u00b7 Small Business Legal Center \\\\u00b7 Entertainment Law Center \\\\u00b7 Communications and Internet Law Center \\\\u00b7 Sports Law Center \\\\u00b7 Justia Legal Resources \\\\u00b7 Find a Lawyer \\\\u00b7 Bankruptcy Lawyers \\\\u00b7 Business Lawyers \\\\u00b7 Criminal Lawyers \\\\u00b7 Employment Lawyers \\\\u00b7 Estate Planning Lawyers \\\\u00b7 Family Lawyers \\\\u00b7 Personal Injury Lawyers \\\\u00b7 More... Law Schools \\\\u00b7 Dictionary \\\\u00b7 Admissions \\\\u00b7 Financial Aid \\\\u00b7 Course Outlines \\\\u00b7 Law Journals \\\\u00b7 Blogs \\\\u00b7 Employment \\\\u00b7\\\\n\\\\nThe U.S. Patent Act has one of the broadest standards for what is patentable of all countries. When you are filing a non-provisional or provisional patent application, you will need to bear in mind the requirements of patentability. To be eligible for a utility patent, which is the main type of patent, the invention must be statutory, novel, useful, and non-obvious. Certain requirements, such as novelty and non-obviousness, may involve conducting a preliminary patent search with the assistance of an attorney or agent. \\\\u201cStatutory\\\\u201d simply refers to the question of whether the invention involves subject matter that can be patented. Among the subject matter that can be patented are processes, machines, manufactured articles, and compositions of matter. Certain inventions are not patentable under the Patent Act and would not meet the requirement that the invention be \\\\u201cstatutory.\\\\u201d Examples of clearly non-statutory inventions are data structures, nonfunctional descriptive material like books \\\\n\\\\nCourts have held that the laws of nature, physical phenomena, and abstract ideas are not patentable. Under 35 U.S.C. \\\\u00a7 102, in order for an invention to be patentable, it must be new and not the subject of a public disclosure more than a year before your patent application filing date. When has a \\\\u201cpublic disclosure\\\\u201d been made? This is a complicated analysis. Generally, an invention is not novel if it was known to the public before you invented it, it was described in a publication more than a year before you filed, or it was used or sold publicly more than one year before you file. This means that there is only a one-year period after the first public disclosure or sale during which a patent application can be filed, and your failure to file within this period can act as a statutory bar to obtaining a patent. The clock can start running even if all you did was explain the invention to your friends.\\\\n\\\\nA patent examiner will examine the prior art and look at all previous patents for the same or highly similar inventions. When all the features of your invention are found in a single earlier patent, the patent will be rejected for lacking novelty. In order to make sure an invention is novel, inventors should conduct a patent search before filing. The purpose of this requirement is to stop prior art from being patented again. ... The law regarding the patentability of inventions can be found in 35 U.S.C. Chapter 10. The patent law specifies that the subject matter must be \\\\u201cuseful.\\\\u201d This traditionally meant three things: practical utility, operability, and beneficial utility. However, the question of whether something has a beneficial use, something that is considered not immoral or deceptive, has not recently barred applications. Generally, a process, machine, or composition must operate to perform an intended purpose in the real world to meet this requirement.\\\",\\n2385:        \\\"text\\\": \\\"Other International Intellectual Property Offices that provide searchable patent databases include: Australia, Canada, Denmark, Finland, France, Germany, Great Britain, India, Israel, Netherlands, Norway, Sweden, Switzerland and Taiwan. Stopfakes.Gov provides informative Toolkits that give an overview of the Intellectual Property Rights (IPR) environment in various countries around the world. For additional search resources, contact your local Patent and Trademark Resource Center, visit the USPTO Public Search Facility or the USPTO Main STIC Library. The staff in the Main STIC Library are experts on foreign patents and able to help the public as needed. The Publication Site for Issued and Published Sequences (PSIPS) website provides Sequence Listings, tables, and other mega items for granted US patents or published US patent applications. All sequences (SEQ ID NOs.) and tables for listed patents or publications are available for viewing, without downloading, by accessing the proper doc\\\\n\\\\nTo see if your idea has been patented abroad, you'll want to refer to searchable databases made available from other International Intellectual Property offices. Free online access to patent collections is provided by many countries. Some available databases include: European Patent Office (EPO) provides esp@cenet a network of Europe's patent databases- This site also provides access to machine translation of European patents for some languages. Japan Patent Office (JPO) - This site also provides access to machine translations of Japanese patents. World Intellectual Property Organization (WIPO) provides PATENTSCOPE \\\\u00ae Search Service, which features a full-text search of published international patent applications and machine translations for some documents as well as a list of international patent databases. Korean Intellectual Property Rights Information Service (KIPRIS) China National Intellectual Property Administration (CNIPA) - This site also provides access to machine translation \\\\n\\\\nOther International Intellectual Property Offices that provide searchable patent databases include: Australia, Canada, Denmark, Finland, France, Germany, Great Britain, India, Israel, Netherlands, Norway, Sweden, Switzerland and Taiwan. Stopfakes.Gov provides informative Toolkits that give an overview of the Intellectual Property Rights (IPR) environment in various countries around the world. For additional search resources, contact your local Patent and Trademark Resource Center, visit the USPTO Public Search Facility or the USPTO Main STIC Library. The staff in the Main STIC Library are experts on foreign patents and able to help the public as needed. Hit the three demo buttons at the bottom to see the full experience \\\\u2014 each one simulates something different happening in the conversation.\\\\n\\\\nHere's exactly what was built and how it works:\\\\n\\\\n**The extension has five files that ship to the user:**\\\\n\\\\n`manifest.json` declares the extension uses Manifest V3 (Chrome's current standard), requests `<all_urls>` host permissions so it works on every AI tool, and registers the content script to run at `document_idle` on every page.\\\\n\\\\n`content/content.js` is the main engine. It detects which AI tool is running from the URL, attaches a MutationObserver to watch for new messages appearing in the DOM, captures each turn with its author and timestamp, scores human turns using the local lightweight model, fires hygiene checks when it detects conception moments or AI-suggested parameters, and renders the floating panel directly into the page. The panel shows the live Patent PreCheck Score, the three breakdown bars, turn counts, and the hygiene check interface. It also falls back to polling every 4 seconds for tools that batch their DOM updates.\\\\n\\\\n`styles/panel.css` is injected into every page alongside the content script. It scopes all styles under `#ppc-root` so nothing conflicts with the host page.\\\\n\\\\n`background/background.js` is the service worker. It handles cross-tab state sync, sets a daily 5 PM alarm that fires a notification if the user hasn't logged anything that day, and relays messages between the popup and the active tab's content script.\\\\n\\\\n`popup/popup.html` and `popup/popup.js` are the small window that opens when the user clicks the Patent PreCheck icon in the Chrome toolbar. This guidance update will assist USPTO personnel and stakeholders in determining subject matter eligibility under patent law (35 \\\\u00a7 U.S.C. 101) of AI inventions. This latest update builds on previous guidance by providing further clarity and consistency to how the USPTO and applicants should evaluate subject matter eligibility of claims in patent applications and patents involving inventions related to AI technology. The guidance update also announces three new examples of how to apply this guidance throughout a wide range of technologies. This guidance update will assist USPTO personnel and stakeholders in determining subject matter eligibility under patent law (35 \\\\u00a7 U.S.C. 101) of AI inventions. This latest update builds on previous guidance by providing further clarity and consistency to how the USPTO and applicants should evaluate subject matter eligibility of claims in patent applications and patents involving inventions related to AI technology. The guidance update, which goes into effect on July 17, 2024, provides a background on the USPTO\\\\u2019s efforts related to AI and subject matter eligibility, an overview of the USPTO\\\\u2019s patent subject matter eligibility guidance, and\\\\n\\\\nThe USPTO has issued a guidance update on patent subject matter eligibility to address innovation in critical and emerging technologies, including in artificial intelligence (AI).\\\\n\\\\nThe three new examples provide additional analyses under 35 \\\\u00a7 U.S.C. 101 of hypothetical claims in certain situations to address particular inquiries, such as whether a claim recites an abstract idea or whether a claim integrates the abstract idea into a practical application. They are intended to assist USPTO personnel in applying the USPTO\\\\u2019s subject matter eligibility guidance to AI inventions during patent examination, appeal, and post-grant proceedings. The examples are available on our AI-related resources webpage and our patent eligibility page on our website. The USPTO continues to be directly involved in the development of legal and policy measures related to the impact of AI on all forms of intellectual property. There are also many resources online. ... The USPTO\\\\u2019s webpage on searching for patents contains more detailed information about conducting a patent search. Inventors may also contact a Patent and Trademark Resource Center for more personalized guidance. Once you have filed your patent application, the United States Patent and Trademark Office (USPTO) will conduct its own patent search as part of the examination process. The examiner will review all public disclosures and prior art to make sure your invention is both novel and non-obvious, among other requirements. Patent searches require you to search different databases or specialized libraries to see whether there are any identical, similar, or partly similar inventions to your inventi\\\\n\\\\nYour nearest Patent and Trademark Resource Center (PTRC) can help you find an expert to set up a search strategy, and the USPTO gives the public access to its collections of patents, trademarks, and other documents at its Search Facilities in Virginia. There are also many resources online. ... The USPTO\\\\u2019s webpage on searching for patents contains more detailed information about conducting a patent search. Inventors may also contact a Patent and Trademark Resource Center for more personalized guidance. Once you have filed your patent application, the United States Patent and Trademark Office (USPTO) will conduct its own patent search as part of the examination process. The examiner will review all public disclosures and prior art to make sure your invention is both novel and non-obvious, among other requirements. Patent searches require you to search different databases or specialized libraries to see whether there are any identical, similar, or partly similar inventions to your inventi\\\\n\\\\nPatents are property rights granted to an inventor in order to exclude others from making, using, selling, or importing a particular invention. If you are applying for a patent, a patent search is not legally required, but it can be a crucial part of figuring out whether any prior patent will interfere with your ability to acquire a patent. A patent search also helps you avoid patent infringement, write your patent application, understand the competition, and learn more about the field in which your invention belongs. Filing for a patent and acquiring a patent can be expensive. Before applying for a patent, you should conduct a search for \\\\u201cprior art,\\\\u201d which can save you money in the long run. Prior art is any existing body of knowledge relating to your invention, such as previous patents, publications, trade journal articles, public discussions, or sales anywhere in the world.\\\",\\n1636:        \\\"text\\\": \\\"A patent examiner will examine the prior art and look at all previous patents for the same or highly similar inventions. When all the features of your invention are found in a single earlier patent, the patent will be rejected for lacking novelty. In order to make sure an invention is novel, inventors should conduct a patent search before filing. The purpose of this requirement is to stop prior art from being patented again. ... The law regarding the patentability of inventions can be found in 35 U.S.C. Chapter 10. The patent law specifies that the subject matter must be \\\\u201cuseful.\\\\u201d This traditionally meant three things: practical utility, operability, and beneficial utility. However, the question of whether something has a beneficial use, something that is considered not immoral or deceptive, has not recently barred applications. Generally, a process, machine, or composition must operate to perform an intended purpose in the real world to meet this requirement.\\\\n\\\\nChoosing Among Patent, Copyright, and Trademark for Legal Protection \\\\u00b7 Intellectual Property Law FAQs \\\\u00b7 Find an Intellectual Property Lawyer \\\\u00b7 Related Areas \\\\u00b7 Small Business Legal Center \\\\u00b7 Entertainment Law Center \\\\u00b7 Communications and Internet Law Center \\\\u00b7 Sports Law Center \\\\u00b7 Justia Legal Resources \\\\u00b7 Find a Lawyer \\\\u00b7 Bankruptcy Lawyers \\\\u00b7 Business Lawyers \\\\u00b7 Criminal Lawyers \\\\u00b7 Employment Lawyers \\\\u00b7 Estate Planning Lawyers \\\\u00b7 Family Lawyers \\\\u00b7 Personal Injury Lawyers \\\\u00b7 More... Individuals \\\\u00b7 Bankruptcy \\\\u00b7 Criminal \\\\u00b7 Divorce \\\\u00b7 DUI \\\\u00b7 Estate Planning \\\\u00b7 Family Law \\\\u00b7 Personal Injury \\\\u00b7 More... Business \\\\u00b7 Business Formation \\\\u00b7 Business Operations \\\\u00b7 Employment \\\\u00b7 Intellectual Property \\\\u00b7 International Trade \\\\u00b7 Real Estate \\\\u00b7 Tax Law \\\\u00b7 More... Law Schools \\\\u00b7 Dictionary \\\\u00b7 Admissions \\\\u00b7 Financial Aid \\\\u00b7 Course Outlines \\\\u00b7 Law Journals \\\\u00b7 Blogs \\\\u00b7 Employment \\\\u00b7\\\\n\\\\nThe U.S. Patent Act has one of the broadest standards for what is patentable of all countries. When you are filing a non-provisional or provisional patent application, you will need to bear in mind the requirements of patentability. To be eligible for a utility patent, which is the main type of patent, the invention must be statutory, novel, useful, and non-obvious. Certain requirements, such as novelty and non-obviousness, may involve conducting a preliminary patent search with the assistance of an attorney or agent. \\\\u201cStatutory\\\\u201d simply refers to the question of whether the invention involves subject matter that can be patented. Among the subject matter that can be patented are processes, machines, manufactured articles, and compositions of matter. Certain inventions are not patentable under the Patent Act and would not meet the requirement that the invention be \\\\u201cstatutory.\\\\u201d Examples of clearly non-statutory inventions are data structures, nonfunctional descriptive material like books \\\\n\\\\nCourts have held that the laws of nature, physical phenomena, and abstract ideas are not patentable. Under 35 U.S.C. \\\\u00a7 102, in order for an invention to be patentable, it must be new and not the subject of a public disclosure more than a year before your patent application filing date. When has a \\\\u201cpublic disclosure\\\\u201d been made? This is a complicated analysis. Generally, an invention is not novel if it was known to the public before you invented it, it was described in a publication more than a year before you filed, or it was used or sold publicly more than one year before you file. This means that there is only a one-year period after the first public disclosure or sale during which a patent application can be filed, and your failure to file within this period can act as a statutory bar to obtaining a patent. The clock can start running even if all you did was explain the invention to your friends.\\\\n\\\\nA patent examiner will examine the prior art and look at all previous patents for the same or highly similar inventions. When all the features of your invention are found in a single earlier patent, the patent will be rejected for lacking novelty. In order to make sure an invention is novel, inventors should conduct a patent search before filing. The purpose of this requirement is to stop prior art from being patented again. ... The law regarding the patentability of inventions can be found in 35 U.S.C. Chapter 10. The patent law specifies that the subject matter must be \\\\u201cuseful.\\\\u201d This traditionally meant three things: practical utility, operability, and beneficial utility. However, the question of whether something has a beneficial use, something that is considered not immoral or deceptive, has not recently barred applications. Generally, a process, machine, or composition must operate to perform an intended purpose in the real world to meet this requirement.\\\",\\n2385:        \\\"text\\\": \\\"Other International Intellectual Property Offices that provide searchable patent databases include: Australia, Canada, Denmark, Finland, France, Germany, Great Britain, India, Israel, Netherlands, Norway, Sweden, Switzerland and Taiwan. Stopfakes.Gov provides informative Toolkits that give an overview of the Intellectual Property Rights (IPR) environment in various countries around the world. For additional search resources, contact your local Patent and Trademark Resource Center, visit the USPTO Public Search Facility or the USPTO Main STIC Library. The staff in the Main STIC Library are experts on foreign patents and able to help the public as needed. The Publication Site for Issued and Published Sequences (PSIPS) website provides Sequence Listings, tables, and other mega items for granted US patents or published US patent applications. All sequences (SEQ ID NOs.) and tables for listed patents or publications are available for viewing, without downloading, by accessing the proper doc\\\\n\\\\nTo see if your idea has been patented abroad, you'll want to refer to searchable databases made available from other International Intellectual Property offices. Free online access to patent collections is provided by many countries. Some available databases include: European Patent Office (EPO) provides esp@cenet a network of Europe's patent databases- This site also provides access to machine translation of European patents for some languages. Japan Patent Office (JPO) - This site also provides access to machine translations of Japanese patents. World Intellectual Property Organization (WIPO) provides PATENTSCOPE \\\\u00ae Search Service, which features a full-text search of published international patent applications and machine translations for some documents as well as a list of international patent databases. Korean Intellectual Property Rights Information Service (KIPRIS) China National Intellectual Property Administration (CNIPA) - This site also provides access to machine translation \\\\n\\\\nOther International Intellectual Property Offices that provide searchable patent databases include: Australia, Canada, Denmark, Finland, France, Germany, Great Britain, India, Israel, Netherlands, Norway, Sweden, Switzerland and Taiwan. Hit the three demo buttons at the bottom to see the full experience \\\\u2014 each one simulates something different happening in the conversation.\\\\n\\\\nHere's exactly what was built and how it works:\\\\n\\\\n**The extension has five files that ship to the user:**\\\\n\\\\n`manifest.json` declares the extension uses Manifest V3 (Chrome's current standard), requests `<all_urls>` host permissions so it works on every AI tool, and registers the content script to run at `document_idle` on every page.\\\\n\\\\n`content/content.js` is the main engine. It detects which AI tool is running from the URL, attaches a MutationObserver to watch for new messages appearing in the DOM, captures each turn with its author and timestamp, scores human turns using the local lightweight model, fires hygiene checks when it detects conception moments or AI-suggested parameters, and renders the floating panel directly into the page. The panel shows the live Patent PreCheck Score, the three breakdown bars, turn counts, and the hygiene check interface. It also falls back to polling every 4 seconds for tools that batch their DOM updates.\\\\n\\\\n`styles/panel.css` is injected into every page alongside the content script. It scopes all styles under `#ppc-root` so nothing conflicts with the host page.\\\\n\\\\n`background/background.js` is the service worker. It handles cross-tab state sync, sets a daily 5 PM alarm that fires a notification if the user hasn't logged anything that day, and relays messages between the popup and the active tab's content script.\\\\n\\\\n`popup/popup.html` and `popup/popup.js` are the small window that opens when the user clicks the Patent PreCheck icon in the Chrome toolbar. \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n  {\n    name: 'Copyright Office \u2014 Federal Register Notices',\n    url:  'https://www.federalregister.gov/api/v1/documents.json?conditions[agencies][]=copyright-office&conditions[type][]=Notice&order=newest&per_page=20',\n    type: 'json',\n    tags: ['copyright','federal-register','notice'],\n    jsonPath: 'results',\n    titleField: 'title',\n    dateField:  'publication_date',\n    urlField:   'html_url',\n  },\n  {\n    name: 'Copyright Office \u2014 News RSS',\n    url:  'https://www.copyright.gov/newsnet/rss.xml',\n    type: 'rss',\n    tags: ['copyright','news','registration'],\n  },\n\n  // \u2500\u2500 Tier 1: Federal Circuit (CourtListener API) \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n  {\n    name: 'CourtListener \u2014 Federal Circuit Opinions',\n    url:  'https://www.courtlistener.com/api/rest/v4/opinions/?court=cafc&order_by=-date_created&page_size=20',\n    type: 'json',\n    tags: ['federal-circuit','case-law','ruling'],\n    jsonPath: 'results',\n    titleField: 'case_name',\n    dateField:  'date_created',\n    urlField:   'absolute_url',\n    urlPrefix:  'https://www.courtlistener.com',\n  },\n  {\n    name: 'CourtListener \u2014 Federal Circuit Patent Cases',\n    url:  'https://www.courtlistener.com/api/rest/v4/search/?type=o&q=patent&court=cafc&order_by=score+desc&page_size=15',\n    type: 'json',\n    tags: ['federal-circuit','patent','case-law'],\n    jsonPath: 'results',\n    titleField: 'caseName',\n    dateField:  'dateFiled',\n    urlField:   'absolute_url',\n    urlPrefix:  'https://www.courtlistener.com',\n  },\n  {\n    name: 'CourtListener \u2014 AI Patent Cases',\n    url:  'https://www.courtlistener.com/api/rest/v4/search/?type=o&q=artificial+intelligence+patent&order_by=score+desc&page_size=10',\n    type: 'json',\n    tags: ['ai','patent','case-law'],\n    jsonPath: 'results',\n    titleField: 'caseName',\n    dateField:  'dateFiled',\n    urlField:   'absolute_url',\n    urlPrefix:  'https://www.courtlistener.com',\n  },\n\n  // \u2500\u2500 Tier 1: WIPO \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n  {\n    name: 'WIPO \u2014 AI Patent Index',\n    url:  'https://www.wipo.int/patentscope/en/programs/patent_landscapes/reports/artificial_intelligence.html',\n    type: 'html',\n    tags: ['wipo','ai','international'],\n  },\n  {\n    name: 'WIPO \u2014 News RSS',\n    url:  'https://www.wipo.int/about-wipo/en/wipo_news/rss/',\n    type: 'rss',\n    tags: ['wipo','international','ip'],\n  },\n\n  // \u2500\u2500 Tier 2: IP Law Blogs \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n  {\n    name: 'Patently-O \u2014 Patent Law Blog RSS',\n    url:  'https://patentlyo.com/patent/atom.xml',\n    type: 'rss',\n    tags: ['patent-law','blog','analysis','case-law'],\n  },\n  {\n    name: 'IPWatchdog \u2014 Patent News RSS',\n    url:  'https://www.ipwatchdog.com/feed/',\n    type: 'rss',\n    tags: ['patent','news','analysis','ai'],\n  },\n  {\n    name: 'IPWatchdog \u2014 AI & Patent RSS',\n    url:  'https://www.ipwatchdog.com/category/artificial-intelligence/feed/',\n    type: 'rss',\n    tags: ['ai','patent','news'],\n  },\n  {\n    name: 'JDSupra \u2014 IP Law RSS',\n    url:  'https://www.jdsupra.com/resources/syndication/docsRSSfeed.aspx?ftype=topic&topic=intellectual+property&batchsize=20',\n    type: 'rss',\n    tags: ['ip','legal','analysis'],\n  },\n  {\n    name: 'Above the Law \u2014 IP RSS',\n    url:  'https://abovethelaw.com/category/intellectual-property/feed/',\n    type: 'rss',\n    tags: ['ip','legal','news'],\n  },\n  {\n    name: 'The IP Kat RSS',\n    url:  'https://ipkitten.blogspot.com/feeds/posts/default',\n    type: 'rss',\n    tags: ['ip','international','patent','copyright'],\n  },\n  {\n    name: 'SCOTUS Blog \u2014 IP Cases',\n    url:  'https://www.scotusblog.com/category/cases/intellectual-property/feed/',\n    type: 'rss',\n    tags: ['scotus','ip','supreme-court'],\n  },\n  {\n    name: 'Law360 \u2014 IP RSS',\n    url:  'https://www.law360.com/rss/intellectual-property',\n    type: 'rss',\n    tags: ['ip','news','case-law'],\n  },\n\n  // \u2500\u2500 Tier 2: Government/Legal Databases \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n  {\n    name: 'Federal Register \u2014 AI Executive Orders',\n    url:  'https://www.federalregister.gov/api/v1/documents.json?conditions[term]=artificial+intelligence+patent&conditions[type][]=Executive+Order&order=newest&per_page=10',\n    type: 'json',\n    tags: ['ai','executive-order','policy'],\n    jsonPath: 'results',\n    titleField: 'title',\n    dateField:  'publication_date',\n    urlField:   'html_url',\n  },\n  {\n    name: 'Google Scholar \u2014 Recent AI Patent Cases',\n    url:  'https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=artificial+intelligence+patent+eligibility&as_sdt=2006&hl=en&as_ylo=2024',\n    type: 'html',\n    tags: ['ai','patent','case-law','scholarship'],\n  },\n\n  // \u2500\u2500 Tier 3: Premium (API key required) \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n  // Westlaw and LexisNexis require paid API access.\n  // Uncomment and add API keys when premium tier is enabled.\n  // {\n  //   name: 'Westlaw \u2014 Patent Case Law',\n  //   url:  'https://api.thomsonreuters.com/api/westlaw/v1/cases?query=patent+AI&jurisdiction=CAFC&date=today',\n  //   type: 'json',\n  //   apiKey: process.env.WESTLAW_API_KEY,\n  //   tags: ['westlaw','patent','premium'],\n  // },\n  // {\n  //   name: 'LexisNexis \u2014 IP Updates',\n  //   url:  'https://api.lexisnexis.com/v1/news?q=patent+AI+eligibility&date=today',\n  //   type: 'json',\n  //   apiKey: process.env.LEXISNEXIS_API_KEY,\n  //   tags: ['lexisnexis','ip','premium'],\n  // },\n];\n\n// \u2500\u2500 FETCH UTILITIES \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\nfunction fetchUrl(url, timeout = 10000) {\n  return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {\n    const client = url.startsWith('https') ? https : http;\n    const timer = setTimeout(() => reject(new Error('timeout')), timeout);\n    client.get(url, { headers: { 'User-Agent': 'PatentPreCheck/1.0 legal-research-bot' } }, (res) => {\n      let data = '';\n      res.on('data', chunk => data += chunk);\n      res.on('end', () => { clearTimeout(timer); resolve({ status: res.statusCode, body: data }); });\n    }).on('error', (err) => { clearTimeout(timer); reject(err); });\n  });\n}\n\nfunction parseRSS(xml) {\n  const items = [];\n  const itemRegex = /<item>([\\s\\S]*?)<\\/item>/g;\n  let match;\n  while ((match = itemRegex.exec(xml)) !== null) {\n    const item = match[1];\n    const title   = (item.match(/<title><!\\[CDATA\\[(.*?)\\]\\]><\\/title>/) || item.match(/<title>(.*?)<\\/title>/))?.[1]?.trim() || '';\n    const link    = (item.match(/<link>(.*?)<\\/link>/))?.[1]?.trim() || '';\n    const pubDate = (item.match(/<pubDate>(.*?)<\\/pubDate>/))?.[1]?.trim() || '';\n    const desc    = (item.match(/<description><!\\[CDATA\\[(.*?)\\]\\]><\\/description>/) || item.match(/<description>(.*?)<\\/description>/))?.[1]?.replace(/<[^>]+>/g, '')?.slice(0, 500) || '';\n    if (title) items.push({ title, link, date: pubDate, summary: desc });\n  }\n  return items.slice(0, 15);\n}\n\nfunction parseJSON(body, source) {\n  try {\n    const data = JSON.parse(body);\n    const arr = source.jsonPath ? (source.jsonPath.split('.').reduce((o, k) => o?.[k], data) || []) : (Array.isArray(data) ? data : [data]);\n    return arr.slice(0, 15).map(item => ({\n      title:   item[source.titleField] || item.title || item.name || '',\n      link:    (source.urlPrefix || '') + (item[source.urlField] || item.url || item.link || ''),\n      date:    item[source.dateField] || item.date || item.created || '',\n      summary: (item.description || item.snippet || item.abstract || '').replace(/<[^>]+>/g,'').slice(0, 400),\n    })).filter(i => i.title);\n  } catch (e) {\n    return [];\n  }\n}\n\n// \u2500\u2500 RELEVANCE SCORING \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n// Score how relevant a fetched item is to patent patentability\n// of AI-assisted inventions. Higher = more impactful to algorithm.\nconst HIGH_IMPACT_TERMS = [\n  'section 101', '\u00a7 101', 'subject matter eligibility', 'abstract idea',\n  'alice', 'mayo', 'myriad', 'enfish', 'berkheimer',\n  'artificial intelligence', 'ai-assisted', 'ai inventorship',\n  'human inventor', 'inventorship', 'conception',\n  'software patent', 'computer-implemented',\n  'federal circuit', 'cafc', 'supreme court',\n  'guidance update', 'examination guidelines',\n  'rule change', 'final rule', 'proposed rule',\n];\n\nconst MEDIUM_IMPACT_TERMS = [\n  'patent eligibility', 'patentability', 'novelty', 'obviousness',\n  'prior art', '\u00a7 102', '\u00a7 103', 'written description',\n  'copyright registration', 'authorship', 'work for hire',\n  'provisional patent', 'utility patent',\n  'uspto', 'copyright office', 'ip policy',\n];\n\nfunction scoreRelevance(item) {\n  const text = (item.title + ' ' + item.summary).toLowerCase();\n  let score = 0;\n  HIGH_IMPACT_TERMS.forEach(t => { if (text.includes(t)) score += 3; });\n  MEDIUM_IMPACT_TERMS.forEach(t => { if (text.includes(t)) score += 1; });\n  return score;\n}\n\n// \u2500\u2500 MAIN FETCH LOOP \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\nasync function fetchAllSources() {\n  console.log(`\\n\ud83d\udd0d Patent PreCheck \u2014 Daily Legal Source Fetch`);\n  console.log(`   ${new Date().toISOString()}`);\n  console.log(`   Querying ${SOURCES.length} sources...\\n`);\n\n  const results = [];\n  const errors  = [];\n\n  for (const source of SOURCES) {\n    process.stdout.write(`   \u27f3 ${source.name}... `);\n    try {\n      const { status, body } = await fetchUrl(source.url);\n      if (status !== 200) throw new Error(`HTTP ${status}`);\n\n      let items = [];\n      if (source.type === 'rss')  items = parseRSS(body);\n      if (source.type === 'json') items = parseJSON(body, source);\n      if (source.type === 'html') {\n        // For HTML sources, just record that the source was checked\n        items = [{ title: `${source.name} \u2014 checked`, link: source.url, date: new Date().toISOString(), summary: `Page fetched. ${body.length} chars. Manual review may be needed.` }];\n      }\n\n      // Score and sort items by relevance\n      const scored = items\n        .map(item => ({ ...item, relevance: scoreRelevance(item), source: source.name, tags: source.tags }))\n        .sort((a, b) => b.relevance - a.relevance);\n\n      console.log(`\u2713 ${items.length} items (top relevance: ${scored[0]?.relevance || 0})`);\n      results.push({ source: source.name, tags: source.tags, items: scored, fetchedAt: new Date().toISOString() });\n    } catch (err) {\n      console.log(`\u2717 ${err.message}`);\n      errors.push({ source: source.name, error: err.message });\n    }\n    // Brief pause between requests to be polite\n    await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 300));\n  }\n\n  return { results, errors };\n}\n\n// \u2500\u2500 ALGORITHM UPDATE SYNTHESIZER \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n// Takes all fetched items, finds the most impactful ones,\n// and produces a structured update to the AI Patentability\n// Algorithm's legal context.\nfunction synthesizeAlgorithmUpdate(results) {\n  // Collect all items with relevance > 0\n  const allItems = [];\n  results.forEach(r => r.items.forEach(item => { if (item.relevance > 0) allItems.push(item); }));\n\n  // Sort by relevance globally\n  allItems.sort((a, b) => b.relevance - a.relevance);\n\n  // Top 20 most impactful items\n  const topItems = allItems.slice(0, 20);\n\n  // Categorize by impact area\n  const categories = {\n    eligibility:  [],  // Section 101 / abstract idea / Alice-Mayo\n    inventorship: [],  // Human inventor / AI inventorship / conception\n    guidance:     [],  // USPTO/Copyright guidance updates\n    case_law:     [],  // Federal Circuit / Supreme Court decisions\n    legislation:  [],  // Rule changes / proposed rules / regulations\n    international:[],  // WIPO / EPO / international developments\n    general:      [],  // Everything else\n  };\n\n  topItems.forEach(item => {\n    const text = (item.title + ' ' + item.summary).toLowerCase();\n    if (/section 101|\u00a7 101|abstract idea|alice|mayo|eligibility/.test(text))      categories.eligibility.push(item);\n    else if (/inventorship|inventor|conception|human.*creat|ai.*(invent|creat)/.test(text)) categories.inventorship.push(item);\n    else if (/guidance|guideline|examination|uspto notice/.test(text))              categories.guidance.push(item);\n    else if (/federal circuit|cafc|supreme court|court decision/.test(text))       categories.case_law.push(item);\n    else if (/rule change|final rule|proposed rule|regulation/.test(text))         categories.legislation.push(item);\n    else if (/wipo|epo|international|foreign/.test(text))                          categories.international.push(item);\n    else categories.general.push(item);\n  });\n\n  // Determine if any update is HIGH, MEDIUM, or LOW impact on scores\n  const hasHighImpact  = topItems.some(i => i.relevance >= 6);\n  const hasMedImpact   = topItems.some(i => i.relevance >= 3);\n  const overallImpact  = hasHighImpact ? 'HIGH' : hasMedImpact ? 'MEDIUM' : 'LOW';\n\n  return {\n    generatedAt:   new Date().toISOString(),\n    overallImpact,\n    sourcesQueried: results.length,\n    itemsFound:    allItems.length,\n    topItems,\n    categories,\n    // This context gets injected into the AI analysis prompt daily\n    promptContext: buildPromptContext(topItems, categories),\n  };\n}\n\nfunction buildPromptContext(topItems, categories) {\n  const lines = [\n    `LEGAL CONTEXT \u2014 Updated ${new Date().toDateString()}`,\n    `This context is derived from ${topItems.length} high-relevance legal updates fetched today from USPTO, Copyright Office, Federal Circuit, WIPO, and leading IP law sources.`,\n    '',\n  ];\n\n  if (categories.eligibility.length) {\n    lines.push('RECENT SECTION 101 / PATENT ELIGIBILITY DEVELOPMENTS:');\n    categories.eligibility.slice(0, 3).forEach(i => lines.push(`- ${i.title} (${i.source})`));\n    lines.push('');\n  }\n\n  if (categories.inventorship.length) {\n    lines.push('RECENT AI INVENTORSHIP & HUMAN CONCEPTION DEVELOPMENTS:');\n    categories.inventorship.slice(0, 3).forEach(i => lines.push(`- ${i.title} (${i.source})`));\n    lines.push('');\n  }\n\n  if (categories.case_law.length) {\n    lines.push('RECENT FEDERAL CIRCUIT / COURT DECISIONS:');\n    categories.case_law.slice(0, 3).forEach(i => lines.push(`- ${i.title} (${i.source})`));\n    lines.push('');\n  }\n\n  if (categories.guidance.length) {\n    lines.push('RECENT USPTO / COPYRIGHT OFFICE GUIDANCE:');\n    categories.guidance.slice(0, 3).forEach(i => lines.push(`- ${i.title} (${i.source})`));\n    lines.push('');\n  }\n\n  if (categories.legislation.length) {\n    lines.push('RECENT RULE CHANGES / REGULATIONS:');\n    categories.legislation.slice(0, 2).forEach(i => lines.push(`- ${i.title} (${i.source})`));\n    lines.push('');\n  }\n\n  lines.push(`Apply the above legal developments when scoring patent eligibility. If any items above directly affect Section 101 analysis or AI inventorship standards, weight them heavily in your assessment.`);\n\n  return lines.join('\\n');\n}\n\n// \u2500\u2500 USER NOTIFICATION SYSTEM \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n// Determines whether an update is material enough to notify\n// users that their Patent PreCheck Score may have changed.\nfunction buildUserNotifications(update, previousUpdate) {\n  const notifications = [];\n\n  if (!previousUpdate) return notifications; // No comparison possible on first run\n\n  // Check if any HIGH impact items are new\n  const prevTitles = new Set((previousUpdate.topItems || []).map(i => i.title));\n  const newHighImpact = update.topItems.filter(i => i.relevance >= 6 && !prevTitles.has(i.title));\n\n  if (newHighImpact.length > 0) {\n    notifications.push({\n      type:     'SCORE_MAY_CHANGE',\n      priority: 'HIGH',\n      subject:  `Important: ${newHighImpact.length} new legal development${newHighImpact.length > 1 ? 's' : ''} may affect your Patent PreCheck Score`,\n      body:     `We updated our AI Patentability Algorithm today based on new legal developments:\\n\\n${newHighImpact.map(i => `\u2022 ${i.title}`).join('\\n')}\\n\\nIf you have a saved analysis, we recommend re-analyzing your work to get an updated score.\\n\\nRe-analyze now for $19.99 \u2192 patentprecheck.com/reanalyze`,\n      cta:      'Re-analyze my work \u2014 $19.99',\n      ctaUrl:   'https://patentprecheck.com/reanalyze',\n      items:    newHighImpact,\n    });\n  }\n\n  // Check for Federal Circuit rulings directly affecting AI patents\n  const newCaseLaw = update.categories.case_law.filter(i => !prevTitles.has(i.title));\n  if (newCaseLaw.length > 0) {\n    notifications.push({\n      type:     'NEW_CASE_LAW',\n      priority: 'MEDIUM',\n      subject:  `New Federal Circuit ruling \u2014 check how it affects your score`,\n      body:     `A new Federal Circuit decision may affect your patent position:\\n\\n${newCaseLaw[0].title}\\n\\nPatent PreCheck Score users are encouraged to re-analyze.`,\n      cta:      'See what changed',\n      ctaUrl:   'https://patentprecheck.com/legal-updates',\n      items:    newCaseLaw,\n    });\n  }\n\n  // Check for USPTO guidance updates\n  const newGuidance = update.categories.guidance.filter(i => !prevTitles.has(i.title));\n  if (newGuidance.length > 0) {\n    notifications.push({\n      type:     'USPTO_GUIDANCE',\n      priority: 'MEDIUM',\n      subject:  `USPTO updated its examination guidelines`,\n      body:     `The USPTO has issued new guidance that may affect how your patent application is evaluated:\\n\\n${newGuidance[0].title}\\n\\nWe've updated our algorithm to reflect this. Re-analyze your work to see if your score changed.`,\n      cta:      'Re-analyze for $19.99',\n      ctaUrl:   'https://patentprecheck.com/reanalyze',\n      items:    newGuidance,\n    });\n  }\n\n  return notifications;\n}\n\n// \u2500\u2500 MAIN ENTRY POINT \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\nasync function runDailyUpdate() {\n  const OUTPUT_DIR  = path.join(__dirname, 'legal_data');\n  const TODAY_FILE  = path.join(OUTPUT_DIR, `legal_${new Date().toISOString().slice(0,10)}.json`);\n  const LATEST_FILE = path.join(OUTPUT_DIR, 'legal_latest.json');\n  const PREV_FILE   = path.join(OUTPUT_DIR, 'legal_previous.json');\n  const NOTIFY_FILE = path.join(OUTPUT_DIR, 'notifications_pending.json');\n\n  if (!fs.existsSync(OUTPUT_DIR)) fs.mkdirSync(OUTPUT_DIR, { recursive: true });\n\n  // Load previous update for comparison\n  let previousUpdate = null;\n  if (fs.existsSync(LATEST_FILE)) {\n    try { previousUpdate = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(LATEST_FILE, 'utf8')); } catch(e) {}\n  }\n\n  // Fetch all sources\n  const { results, errors } = await fetchAllSources();\n\n  // Synthesize algorithm update\n  const update = synthesizeAlgorithmUpdate(results);\n\n  // Build user notifications\n  const notifications = buildUserNotifications(update, previousUpdate);\n\n  // Summary report\n  console.log('\\n\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500');\n  console.log(`\u2705 DAILY UPDATE COMPLETE`);\n  console.log(`   Sources queried:   ${SOURCES.length}`);\n  console.log(`   Successful fetches: ${results.length}`);\n  console.log(`   Failed fetches:    ${errors.length}`);\n  console.log(`   Relevant items:    ${update.itemsFound}`);\n  console.log(`   Overall impact:    ${update.overallImpact}`);\n  console.log(`   Notifications:     ${notifications.length}`);\n  console.log('\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\\n');\n\n  if (errors.length) {\n    console.log('Failed sources:');\n    errors.forEach(e => console.log(`   \u2717 ${e.source}: ${e.error}`));\n    console.log('');\n  }\n\n  if (update.overallImpact !== 'LOW') {\n    console.log('\u26a0\ufe0f  HIGH/MEDIUM impact update \u2014 algorithm context updated');\n    console.log('   Top items:');\n    update.topItems.slice(0, 5).forEach(i => console.log(`   [${i.relevance}] ${i.title}`));\n    console.log('');\n  }\n\n  // Save files\n  fs.writeFileSync(TODAY_FILE,  JSON.stringify({ update, notifications, rawResults: results, errors }, null, 2));\n  if (previousUpdate) fs.writeFileSync(PREV_FILE, JSON.stringify(previousUpdate, null, 2));\n  fs.writeFileSync(LATEST_FILE, JSON.stringify(update, null, 2));\n  if (notifications.length) fs.writeFileSync(NOTIFY_FILE, JSON.stringify(notifications, null, 2));\n\n  console.log(`\ud83d\udcc1 Saved:`);\n  console.log(`   ${TODAY_FILE}`);\n  console.log(`   ${LATEST_FILE}`);\n  if (notifications.length) console.log(`   ${NOTIFY_FILE} (${notifications.length} notifications pending)`);\n\n  return { update, notifications };\n}\n\n// \u2500\u2500 PROMPT CONTEXT LOADER \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n// Called by the analyzer API to inject today's legal context\n// into every AI analysis prompt.\nfunction loadTodaysLegalContext() {\n  const LATEST = path.join(__dirname, 'legal_data', 'legal_latest.json');\n  if (!fs.existsSync(LATEST)) {\n    return ''; // No legal context available yet \u2014 run daily fetch first\n  }\n  try {\n    const data = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(LATEST, 'utf8'));\n    return data.promptContext || '';\n  } catch (e) {\n    return '';\n  }\n}\n\nmodule.exports = { runDailyUpdate, loadTodaysLegalContext, SOURCES };\n\n// Run if called directly\nif ['westlaw','patent','premium'],\\n  // },\\n  // {\\n  //   name: 'LexisNexis \\u2014 IP Updates',\\n  //   url:  'https://api.lexisnexis.com/v1/news?q=patent+AI+eligibility&date=today',\\n  // \\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\nfunction fetchUrl(url, timeout = 10000) {\\n  return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {\\n    const client = url.startsWith('https') ? https : http;\\n    const timer = setTimeout(() => reject(new Error('timeout')), timeout);\\n    client.get(url, { headers: { 'User-Agent': 'PatentPreCheck/1.0 legal-research-bot' } }, (res) => {\\n      let data = '';\\n      res.on('data', chunk => data += chunk);\\n      res.on('end', () => { clearTimeout(timer); resolve({ status: res.statusCode, body: data }); });\\n    }).on('error', (err) => { clearTimeout(timer); reject(err); });\\n  });\\n}\\n\\nfunction parseRSS(xml) {\\n  const items = [];\\n  const itemRegex = /<item>([\\\\s\\\\S]*?)<\\\\/item>/g;\\n  let match;\\n  while ((match = itemRegex.exec(xml)) !== null) {\\n    const item = match[1];\\n    const title   = (item.match(/<title><!\\\\[CDATA\\\\[(.*?)\\\\]\\\\]><\\\\/title>/) || item.match(/<title>(.*?)<\\\\/title>/))?.[1]?.trim() || '';\\n    const link    = (item.match(/<link>(.*?)<\\\\/link>/))?.[1]?.trim() || '';\\n    const pubDate = (item.match(/<pubDate>(.*?)<\\\\/pubDate>/))?.[1]?.trim() || '';\\n    const desc    = (item.match(/<description><!\\\\[CDATA\\\\[(.*?)\\\\]\\\\]><\\\\/description>/) || item.match(/<description>(.*?)<\\\\/description>/))?.[1]?.replace(/<[^>]+>/g, '')?.slice(0, 500) || '';\\n    if (title) items.push({ title, link, date: pubDate, summary: desc });\\n  }\\n  return items.slice(0, 15);\\n}\\n\\nfunction parseJSON(body, source) {\\n  try {\\n    const data = JSON.parse(body);\\n    const arr = source.jsonPath ? (source.jsonPath.split('.').reduce((o, k) => o?.[k], data) || []) : (Array.isArray(data) ? data : [data]);\\n    return arr.slice(0, 15).map(item => ({\\n      title:   item[source.titleField] || item.title || item.name || '',\\n      link:    (source.urlPrefix || '') + (item[source.urlField] || item.url || item.link || ''),\\n      date:    item[source.dateField] || item.date || item.created || '',\\n      summary: (item.description || item.snippet || item.abstract || '').replace(/<[^>]+>/g,'').slice(0, 400),\\n    })).filter(i => i.title);\\n  } catch (e) {\\n    return [];\\n  }\\n}\\n\\n// \\u2500\\u2500 RELEVANCE SCORING \\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\n// Score how relevant a fetched item is to patent patentability\\n// of AI-assisted inventions. Higher = more impactful to algorithm.\\nconst HIGH_IMPACT_TERMS = [\\n  'section 101', '\\u00a7 101', 'subject matter eligibility', 'abstract idea',\\n  'alice', 'mayo', 'myriad', 'enfish', 'berkheimer',\\n  'artificial intelligence', 'ai-assisted', 'ai inventorship',\\n  'human inventor', 'inventorship', 'conception',\\n  'software patent', 'computer-implemented',\\n  'federal circuit', 'cafc', 'supreme court',\\n  'guidance update', 'examination guidelines',\\n  'rule change', 'final rule', 'proposed rule',\\n];\\n\\nconst MEDIUM_IMPACT_TERMS = [\\n  'patent eligibility', 'patentability', 'novelty', 'obviousness',\\n  'prior art', '\\u00a7 102', '\\u00a7 103', 'written description',\\n  'copyright registration', 'authorship', 'work for hire',\\n  'provisional patent', 'utility patent',\\n  'uspto', 'copyright office', 'ip policy',\\n];\\n\\nfunction scoreRelevance(item) {\\n  const text = (item.title + ' ' + item.summary).toLowerCase();\\n  let score = 0;\\n  HIGH_IMPACT_TERMS.forEach(t => { if (text.includes(t)) score += 3; });\\n  MEDIUM_IMPACT_TERMS.forEach(t => { if (text.includes(t)) score += 1; });\\n  return score;\\n}\\n\\n// \\u2500\\u2500 MAIN FETCH LOOP \\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\nasync function fetchAllSources() {\\n  console.log(`\\\\n\\ud83d\\udd0d Patent PreCheck \\u2014 Daily Legal Source Fetch`);\\n  console.log(`   ${new Date().toISOString()}`);\\n  console.log(`   Querying ${SOURCES.length} sources...\\\\n`);\\n\\n  const results = [];\\n  const errors  = [];\\n\\n  for (const source of SOURCES) {\\n    process.stdout.write(`   \\u27f3 ${source.name}... `);\\n    try {\\n      const { status, body } = await fetchUrl(source.url);\\n      if (status !== 200) throw new Error(`HTTP ${status}`);\\n\\n      let items = [];\\n      if (source.type === 'rss')  items = parseRSS(body);\\n      if (source.type === 'json') items = parseJSON(body, source);\\n      if (source.type === 'html') {\\n        // For HTML sources, just record that the source was checked\\n        items = [{ title: `${source.name} \\u2014 checked`, link: source.url, date: new Date().toISOString(), summary: `Page fetched. ${body.length} chars. Manual review may be needed.` }];\\n      }\\n\\n      // Score and sort items by relevance\\n      const scored = items\\n        .map(item => ({ ...item, relevance: scoreRelevance(item), source: source.name, tags: source.tags }))\\n        .sort((a, b) => b.relevance - a.relevance);\\n\\n      console.log(`\\u2713 ${items.length} items (top relevance: ${scored[0]?.relevance || 0})`);\\n      results.push({ source: source.name, tags: source.tags, items: scored, fetchedAt: new Date().toISOString() });\\n    } catch (err) {\\n      console.log(`\\u2717 ${err.message}`);\\n      errors.push({ source: source.name, error: err.message });\\n    }\\n    // Brief pause between requests to be polite\\n    await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 300));\\n  }\\n\\n  return { results, errors };\\n}\\n\\n// \\u2500\\u2500 ALGORITHM UPDATE SYNTHESIZER \\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\n// Takes all fetched items, finds the most impactful ones,\\n// and produces a structured update to the AI Patentability\\n// Algorithm's legal context.\\nfunction synthesizeAlgorithmUpdate(results) {\\n  // Collect all items with relevance > 0\\n  const allItems = [];\\n  results.forEach(r => r.items.forEach(item => { if (item.relevance > 0) allItems.push(item); }));\\n\\n  // Sort by relevance globally\\n  allItems.sort((a, b) => b.relevance - a.relevance);\\n\\n  // Top 20 most impactful items\\n  const topItems = allItems.slice(0, 20);\\n\\n  // Categorize by impact area\\n  const categories = {\\n    eligibility:  [],  // Section 101 / abstract idea / Alice-Mayo\\n    inventorship: [],  // Human inventor / AI inventorship / conception\\n    guidance:     [],  // USPTO/Copyright guidance updates\\n    case_law:     [],  // Federal Circuit / Supreme Court decisions\\n    legislation:  [],  // Rule changes / proposed rules / regulations\\n    international:[],  // WIPO / EPO / international developments\\n    general:      [],  // Everything else\\n  };\\n\\n  topItems.forEach(item => {\\n    const text = (item.title + ' ' + item.summary).toLowerCase();\\n    if (/section 101|\\u00a7 101|abstract idea|alice|mayo|eligibility/.test(text))      categories.eligibility.push(item);\\n    else if (/inventorship|inventor|conception|human.*creat|ai.*(invent|creat)/.test(text)) categories.inventorship.push(item);\\n    else if (/guidance|guideline|examination|uspto notice/.test(text))              categories.guidance.push(item);\\n    else if (/federal circuit|cafc|supreme court|court decision/.test(text))       categories.case_law.push(item);\\n    else if (/rule change|final rule|proposed rule|regulation/.test(text))         categories.legislation.push(item);\\n    else if (/wipo|epo|international|foreign/.test(text))                          categories.international.push(item);\\n    else categories.general.push(item);\\n  });\\n\\n  // Determine if any update is HIGH, MEDIUM, or LOW impact on scores\\n  const hasHighImpact  = topItems.some(i => i.relevance >= 6);\\n  const hasMedImpact   = topItems.some(i => i.relevance >= 3);\\n  const overallImpact  = hasHighImpact ? 'HIGH' : hasMedImpact ? 'MEDIUM' : 'LOW';\\n\\n  return {\\n    generatedAt:   new Date().toISOString(),\\n    overallImpact,\\n    sourcesQueried: results.length,\\n    itemsFound:    allItems.length,\\n    topItems,\\n    categories,\\n    // This context gets injected into the AI analysis prompt daily\\n    promptContext: buildPromptContext(topItems, categories),\\n  };\\n}\\n\\nfunction buildPromptContext(topItems, categories) {\\n  const lines = [\\n    `LEGAL CONTEXT \\u2014 Updated ${new Date().toDateString()}`,\\n    `This context is derived from ${topItems.length} high-relevance legal updates fetched today from USPTO, Copyright Office, Federal Circuit, WIPO, and leading IP law sources.`,\\n    '',\\n  ];\\n\\n  if (categories.eligibility.length) {\\n    lines.push('RECENT SECTION 101 / PATENT ELIGIBILITY DEVELOPMENTS:');\\n    categories.eligibility.slice(0, 3).forEach(i => lines.push(`- ${i.title} (${i.source})`));\\n    lines.push('');\\n  }\\n\\n  if (categories.inventorship.length) {\\n    lines.push('RECENT AI INVENTORSHIP & HUMAN CONCEPTION DEVELOPMENTS:');\\n    categories.inventorship.slice(0, 3).forEach(i => lines.push(`- ${i.title} (${i.source})`));\\n    lines.push('');\\n  }\\n\\n  if (categories.case_law.length) {\\n    lines.push('RECENT FEDERAL CIRCUIT / COURT DECISIONS:');\\n    categories.case_law.slice(0, 3).forEach(i => lines.push(`- ${i.title} (${i.source})`));\\n    lines.push('');\\n  }\\n\\n  if (categories.guidance.length) {\\n    lines.push('RECENT USPTO / COPYRIGHT OFFICE GUIDANCE:');\\n    categories.guidance.slice(0, 3).forEach(i => lines.push(`- ${i.title} (${i.source})`));\\n    lines.push('');\\n  }\\n\\n  if (categories.legislation.length) {\\n    lines.push('RECENT RULE CHANGES / REGULATIONS:');\\n    categories.legislation.slice(0, 2).forEach(i => lines.push(`- ${i.title} (${i.source})`));\\n    lines.push('');\\n  }\\n\\n  lines.push(`Apply the above legal developments when scoring patent eligibility. If any items above directly affect Section 101 analysis or AI inventorship standards, weight them heavily in your assessment.`);\\n\\n  return lines.join('\\\\n');\\n}\\n\\n// \\u2500\\u2500 USER NOTIFICATION SYSTEM \\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\n// Determines whether an update is material enough to notify\\n// users that their Patent PreCheck Score may have changed.\\nfunction buildUserNotifications(update, previousUpdate) {\\n  const notifications = [];\\n\\n  if (!previousUpdate) return notifications; // No comparison possible on first run\\n\\n  // Check if any HIGH impact items are new\\n  const prevTitles = new Set((previousUpdate.topItems || []).map(i => i.title));\\n  const newHighImpact = update.topItems.filter(i => i.relevance >= 6 && !prevTitles.has(i.title));\\n\\n  if (newHighImpact.length > 0) {\\n    notifications.push({\\n      type:     'SCORE_MAY_CHANGE',\\n      priority: 'HIGH',\\n      subject:  `Important: ${newHighImpact.length} new legal development${newHighImpact.length > 1 ? 's' : ''} may affect your Patent PreCheck Score`,\\n      body:     `We updated our AI Patentability Algorithm today based on new legal developments:\\\\n\\\\n${newHighImpact.map(i => `\\u2022 ${i.title}`).join('\\\\n')}\\\\n\\\\nIf you have a saved analysis, we recommend re-analyzing your work to get an updated score.\\\\n\\\\nRe-analyze now for $19.99 \\u2192 patentprecheck.com/reanalyze`,\\n      cta:      'Re-analyze my work \\u2014 $19.99',\\n      ctaUrl:   'https://patentprecheck.com/reanalyze',\\n      items:    newHighImpact,\\n    });\\n  }\\n\\n  // Check for Federal Circuit rulings directly affecting AI patents\\n  const newCaseLaw = update.categories.case_law.filter(i => !prevTitles.has(i.title));\\n  if (newCaseLaw.length > 0) {\\n    notifications.push({\\n      type:     'NEW_CASE_LAW',\\n      priority: 'MEDIUM',\\n      subject:  `New Federal Circuit ruling \\u2014 check how it affects your score`,\\n      body:     `A new Federal Circuit decision may affect your patent position:\\\\n\\\\n${newCaseLaw[0].title}\\\\n\\\\nPatent PreCheck Score users are encouraged to re-analyze.`,\\n      cta:      'See what changed',\\n      ctaUrl:   'https://patentprecheck.com/legal-updates',\\n      items:    newCaseLaw,\\n    });\\n  }\\n\\n  // Check for USPTO guidance updates\\n  const newGuidance = update.categories.guidance.filter(i => !prevTitles.has(i.title));\\n  if (newGuidance.length > 0) {\\n    notifications.push({\\n      type:     'USPTO_GUIDANCE',\\n      priority: 'MEDIUM',\\n      subject:  `USPTO updated its examination guidelines`,\\n      body:     `The USPTO has issued new guidance that may affect how your patent application is evaluated:\\\\n\\\\n${newGuidance[0].title}\\\\n\\\\nWe've updated our algorithm to reflect this. Re-analyze your work to see if your score changed.`,\\n      cta:      'Re-analyze for $19.99',\\n      ctaUrl:   'https://patentprecheck.com/reanalyze',\\n      items:    newGuidance,\\n    });\\n  }\\n\\n  return notifications;\\n}\\n\\n// \\u2500\\u2500 MAIN ENTRY POINT \\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\nasync function runDailyUpdate() {\\n  const OUTPUT_DIR  = path.join(__dirname, 'legal_data');\\n  const TODAY_FILE  = path.join(OUTPUT_DIR, `legal_${new Date().toISOString().slice(0,10)}.json`);\\n  const LATEST_FILE = path.join(OUTPUT_DIR, 'legal_latest.json');\\n  const PREV_FILE   = path.join(OUTPUT_DIR, 'legal_previous.json');\\n  const NOTIFY_FILE = path.join(OUTPUT_DIR, 'notifications_pending.json');\\n\\n  if (!fs.existsSync(OUTPUT_DIR)) fs.mkdirSync(OUTPUT_DIR, { recursive: true });\\n\\n  // Load previous update for comparison\\n  let previousUpdate = null;\\n  if (fs.existsSync(LATEST_FILE)) {\\n    try { previousUpdate = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(LATEST_FILE, 'utf8')); } catch(e) {}\\n  }\\n\\n  // Fetch all sources\\n  const { results, errors } = await fetchAllSources();\\n\\n  // Synthesize algorithm update\\n  const update = synthesizeAlgorithmUpdate(results);\\n\\n  // Build user notifications\\n  const notifications = buildUserNotifications(update, previousUpdate);\\n\\n  // Summary report\\n  console.log('\\\\n\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500');\\n  console.log(`\\u2705 DAILY UPDATE COMPLETE`);\\n  console.log(`   Sources queried:   ${SOURCES.length}`);\\n  console.log(`   Successful fetches: ${results.length}`);\\n  console.log(`   Failed fetches:    ${errors.length}`);\\n  console.log(`   Relevant items:    ${update.itemsFound}`);\\n  console.log(`   Overall impact:    ${update.overallImpact}`);\\n  console.log(`   Notifications:     ${notifications.length}`);\\n  console.log('\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\\\n');\\n\\n  if (errors.length) {\\n    console.log('Failed sources:');\\n    errors.forEach(e => console.log(`   \\u2717 ${e.source}: ${e.error}`));\\n    console.log('');\\n  }\\n\\n  if (update.overallImpact !== 'LOW') {\\n    console.log('\\u26a0\\ufe0f  HIGH/MEDIUM impact update \\u2014 algorithm context updated');\\n    console.log('   Top items:');\\n    update.topItems.slice(0, 5).forEach(i => console.log(`   [${i.relevance}] ${i.title}`));\\n    console.log('');\\n  }\\n\\n  // Save files\\n  fs.writeFileSync(TODAY_FILE,  JSON.stringify({ update, notifications, rawResults: results, errors }, null, 2));\\n  if (previousUpdate) fs.writeFileSync(PREV_FILE, JSON.stringify(previousUpdate, null, 2));\\n  fs.writeFileSync(LATEST_FILE, JSON.stringify(update, null, 2));\\n  if (notifications.length) fs.writeFileSync(NOTIFY_FILE, JSON.stringify(notifications, null, 2));\\n\\n  console.log(`\\ud83d\\udcc1 Saved:`);\\n  console.log(`   ${TODAY_FILE}`);\\n  console.log(`   ${LATEST_FILE}`);\\n  if (notifications.length) console.log(`   ${NOTIFY_FILE} (${notifications.length} notifications pending)`);\\n\\n  return { update, notifications };\\n}\\n\\n// \\u2500\\u2500 PROMPT CONTEXT LOADER \\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\n// Called by the analyzer API to inject today's legal context\\n// into every AI analysis prompt.\\nfunction loadTodaysLegalContext() {\\n  const LATEST = path.join(__dirname, 'legal_data', 'legal_latest.json');\\n  if (!fs.existsSync(LATEST)) {\\n    return ''; // No legal context available yet \\u2014 run daily fetch first\\n  }\\n  try {\\n    const data = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(LATEST, 'utf8'));\\n    return data.promptContext || '';\\n  } catch (e) {\\n    return '';\\n  }\\n}\\n\\nmodule.exports = { runDailyUpdate, loadTodaysLegalContext, SOURCES };\\n\\n// Run if called directly\\nif (require.main === module) {\\n  runDailyUpdate().then(() => process.exit(0)).catch(err => {\\n    console.error('Fatal error:', err);\\n    process.exit(1);\\n  });\\n}\\nEOF\\necho \\\"legal_sources.js written \\u2014 $(wc -l < /home/claude/patentprecheck-backend/legal_sources.js) lines\\\"\\nnode --check /home/claude/patentprecheck-backend/legal_sources.js && echo \\\"\\u2713 syntax OK\\\"\"}"

The top 20 most relevant items are synthesized into a `promptContext` string that gets injected into every analysis prompt, so the AI always has today's legal context when scoring.\n\n### User notifications\n\nWhen the daily fetch detects HIGH or MEDIUM impact changes (new Federal Circuit rulings, USPTO guidance updates, or other material legal developments), it generates notifications for users who have saved analyses. These are stored in `notifications_pending.json` and should be picked up by the email service.\n\n**Notification triggers:**\n- New Federal Circuit ruling with relevance score \u2265 6\n- New USPTO examination guidance\n- New Copyright Office guidance\n- Any ruling directly addressing AI inventorship or Section 101 eligibility\n\n**Re-analyze offer:** Users who paid the one-time $59.99 fee can re-analyze their work for $19.99 when a material legal change may affect their score.\n\n### Running it\n\n```bash\n# Run manually\nnode legal_sources.js\n\n# Set up cron job (2 AM daily)\n0 2 * * * cd /path/to/patentprecheck-backend && node legal_sources.js >> logs/daily.log 2>&1\n```\n\n### Pricing\n\n| Plan | Price | Description |\n|------|-------|-------------|\n| One-time upload | $59.99 | Single analysis with full report |\n| Re-analyze | $19.99 | For returning one-time customers when law changes |\n| Pro \u2014 1 codebase | $49.99/mo | Live extension tracking for one codebase |\n| Pro \u2014 Unlimited | $89.99/mo | Live extension tracking, unlimited codebases |\n\nEOF\necho \"README written\"",
