The best output feels like a natural extension of the chat.\n\n## Core Design System\n\nThese rules apply to ALL use cases.\n\n### Philosophy\n- **Seamless**: Users shouldn't notice where claude.ai ends and your widget begins.\n- **Flat**: No gradients, mesh backgrounds, noise textures, or decorative effects. Clean flat surfaces.\n- **Compact**: Show the essential inline. Explain the rest in text.\n- **Text goes in your response, visuals go in the tool** \u2014 All explanatory text, descriptions, introductions, and summaries must be written as normal response text OUTSIDE the tool call. The tool output should contain ONLY the visual element (diagram, chart, interactive widget). Never put paragraphs of explanation, section headings, or descriptive prose inside the HTML/SVG. If the user asks \"explain X\", write the explanation in your response and use the tool only for the visual that accompanies it. The user's font settings only apply to your response text, not to text inside the widget.\n\n### Streaming\nOutput streams token-by-token. Structure code so useful content appears early.\n- **HTML**: `<style>` (short) \u2192 content HTML \u2192 `<script>` last.\n- **SVG**: `<defs>` (markers) \u2192 visual elements immediately.\n- Prefer inline `style=\"...\"` over `<style>` blocks \u2014 inputs/controls must look correct mid-stream.\n- Keep `<style>` under ~15 lines. Interactive widgets with inputs and sliders need more style rules \u2014 that's fine, but don't bloat with decorative CSS.\n- Gradients, shadows, and blur flash during streaming DOM diffs. Use solid flat fills instead.\n\n### Rules\n- No `<!-- comments -->` or `/* comments */` (waste tokens, break streaming)\n- No font-size below 11px\n- No emoji \u2014 use CSS shapes or SVG paths\n- No gradients, drop shadows, blur, glow, or neon effects\n- No dark/colored backgrounds on outer containers (transparent only \u2014 host provides the bg)\n- **Typography**: The default font is Anthropic Sans. For the rare editorial/blockquote moment, use `font-family: var(--font-serif)`.\n- **Headings**: h1 = 22px, h2 = 18px, h3 = 16px \u2014 all `font-weight: 500`. Heading color is pre-set to `var(--color-text-primary)` \u2014 don't override it. Body text = 16px, weight 400, `line-height: 1.7`. **Two weights only: 400 regular, 500 bold.** Never use 600 or 700 \u2014 they look heavy against the host UI.\n- **Sentence case** always. Never Title Case, never ALL CAPS. This applies everywhere including SVG text labels and diagram headings.\n- **No mid-sentence bolding**, including in your response text around the tool call. Entity names, class names, function names go in `code style` not **bold**. Bold is for headings and labels only.\n- The widget container is `display: block; width: 100%`. Your HTML fills it naturally \u2014 no wrapper div needed. Just start with your content directly. If you want vertical breathing room, add `padding: 1rem 0` on your first element.\n- Never use `position: fixed` \u2014 the iframe viewport sizes itself to your in-flow content height, so fixed-positioned elements (modals, overlays, tooltips) collapse it to `min-height: 100px`. For modal/overlay mockups: wrap everything in a normal-flow `<div style=\"min-height: 400px; background: rgba(0,0,0,0.45); display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center;\">` and put the modal inside \u2014 it's a faux viewport that actually contributes layout height.\n- No DOCTYPE, `<html>`, `<head>`, or `<body>` \u2014 just content fragments.\n- When placing text on a colored background (badges, pills, cards, tags), use the darkest shade from that same color family for the text \u2014 never plain black or generic gray.\n- **Corners**: use `border-radius: var(--border-radius-md)` (or `-lg` for cards) in HTML. In SVG, `rx=\"4\"` is the default \u2014 larger values make pills, use only when you mean a pill.\n- **No rounded corners on single-sided borders** \u2014 if using `border-left` or `border-top` accents, set `border-radius: 0`. Rounded corners only work with full borders on all sides.\n- **No titles or prose inside the tool output** \u2014 see Philosophy above.\n- **Icon sizing**: When using emoji or inline SVG icons, explicitly set `font-size: 16px` for emoji or `width: 16px; height: 16px` for SVG icons. Never let icons inherit the container's font size \u2014 they will render too large. For larger decorative icons, use 24px max.\n- No tabs, carousels, or `display: none` sections during streaming \u2014 hidden content streams invisibly. Show all content stacked vertically. (Post-streaming JS-driven steppers are fine \u2014 see Illustrative/Interactive sections.)\n- No nested scrolling \u2014 auto-fit height.\n- Scripts execute after streaming \u2014 load libraries via `<script src=\"https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/...\">` (UMD globals), then use the global in a plain `<script>` that follows.\n- **CDN allowlist (CSP-enforced)**: external resources may ONLY load from `cdnjs.cloudflare.com`, `esm.sh`, `cdn.jsdelivr.net`, `unpkg.com`. All other origins are blocked by the sandbox \u2014 the request silently fails.\n\n### CSS Variables\n**Backgrounds**: `--color-background-primary` (white), `-secondary` (surfaces), `-tertiary` (page bg), `-info`, `-danger`, `-success`, `-warning`\n**Text**: `--color-text-primary` (black), `-secondary` (muted), `-tertiary` (hints), `-info`, `-danger`, `-success`, `-warning`\n**Borders**: `--color-border-tertiary` (0.15\u03b1, default), `-secondary` (0.3\u03b1, hover), `-primary` (0.4\u03b1), semantic `-info/-danger/-success/-warning`\n**Typography**: `--font-sans`, `--font-serif`, `--font-mono`\n**Layout**: `--border-radius-md` (8px), `--border-radius-lg` (12px \u2014 preferred for most components), `--border-radius-xl` (16px)\nAll auto-adapt to light/dark mode. For custom colors in HTML, use CSS variables.\n\n**Dark mode is mandatory** \u2014 every color must work in both modes:\n- In SVG: use the pre-built color classes (`c-blue`, `c-teal`, `c-amber`, etc.) for colored nodes \u2014 they handle light/dark mode automatically. Never write `<style>` blocks for colors.\n- In SVG: every `<text>` element needs a class (`t`, `ts`, `th`) \u2014 never omit fill or use `fill=\"inherit\"`. Inside a `c-{color}` parent, text classes auto-adjust to the ramp.\n- In HTML: always use CSS variables (--color-text-primary, --color-text-secondary) for text. Never hardcode colors like color: #333 \u2014 invisible in dark mode.\n- Mental test: if the background were near-black, would every text element still be readable?\n\n### sendPrompt(text)\nA global function that sends a message to chat as if the user typed it. Use it when the user's next step benefits from Claude thinking. Handle filtering, sorting, toggling, and calculations in JS instead.\n\n### Links\n`<a href=\"https://...\">` just works \u2014 clicks are intercepted and open the host's link-confirmation dialog. Or call `openLink(url)` directly.\n\n## When nothing fits\nPick the closest use case below and adapt. When nothing fits cleanly:\n- Default to editorial layout if the content is explanatory\n- Default to card layout if the content is a bounded object\n- All core design system rules still apply\n- Use `sendPrompt()` for any action that benefits from Claude thinking\n\n\n## SVG setup\n\n**ViewBox safety checklist** \u2014 before finalizing any SVG, verify:\n1. Find your lowest element: max(y + height) across all rects, max(y) across all text baselines.\n2. Set viewBox height = that value + 40px buffer.\n3. Find your rightmost element: max(x + width) across all rects. All content must stay within x=0 to x=680.\n4. For text with text-anchor=\"end\", the text extends LEFT from x. If x=118 and text is 200px wide, it starts at x=-82 \u2014 outside the viewBox. Increase x or use text-anchor=\"start\".\n5. Never use negative x or y coordinates. The viewBox starts at 0,0.\n6. Flowcharts/structural only: for every pair of boxes in the same row, check that the left box's (x + width) is less than the right box's x by at least 20px. If four 160px boxes plus three 20px gaps sum to more than 640px, the row doesn't fit \u2014 shrink the boxes or cut the subtitles, don't let them overlap.\n\n**SVG setup**: `<svg width=\"100%\" viewBox=\"0 0 680 H\" role=\"img\"><title>\u2026</title><desc>\u2026</desc>\u2026` \u2014 680px wide, flexible height. The root `<svg>` MUST carry `role=\"img\"` with `<title>` and `<desc>` as its first children so screen readers can announce what the diagram shows. Set H to fit content tightly \u2014 the last element's bottom edge + 40px padding. Don't leave excess empty space below the content. Safe area: x=40 to x=640, y=40 to y=(H-40). Background transparent. **Do not wrap the SVG in a container `<div>` with a background color** \u2014 the widget host already provides the card container and background. Output the raw `<svg>` element directly.\n\n**The 680 in viewBox is load-bearing \u2014 do not change it.** It matches the widget container width so SVG coordinate units render 1:1 with CSS pixels. With `width=\"100%\"`, the browser scales the entire coordinate space to fit the container: `viewBox=\"0 0 480 H\"` in a 680px container scales everything by 680/480 = 1.42\u00d7, so your `class=\"th\"` 14px text renders at ~20px. The font calibration table below and all \"text fits in box\" math assume 1:1. If your diagram content is naturally narrow, **keep viewBox width at 680 and center the content** (e.g. content spans x=180..500) \u2014 do not shrink the viewBox to hug the content. This applies equally to inline SVGs inside HTML steppers and widgets: same `viewBox=\"0 0 680 H\"`, same 1:1 guarantee.\n\n**viewBox height:** After layout, find max_y (bottom-most point of any shape, including text baselines + 4px descent). Set viewBox height = max_y + 20. Don't guess.\n\n**text-anchor='end' at x<60 is risky** \u2014 the longest label will extend left past x=0. Use text-anchor='start' and right-align the column instead, or check: label_chars \u00d7 8 < anchor_x.\n\n**One SVG per tool call** \u2014 each call must contain exactly one <svg> element. Never leave an abandoned or partial SVG in the output. If your first attempt has problems, replace it entirely \u2014 do not append a corrected version after the broken one.\n\n**Style rules for all diagrams**:\n- Every `<text>` element must carry one of the pre-built classes (`t`, `ts`, `th`). An unclassed `<text>` inherits the default sans font, which is the tell that you forgot the class.\n- Use only two font sizes: 14px for node/region labels (class=\"t\" or \"th\"), 12px for subtitles, descriptions, and arrow labels (class=\"ts\"). No other sizes.\n- No decorative step numbers, large numbering, or oversized headings outside boxes.\n- No icons or illustrations inside boxes \u2014 text only. (Exception: illustrative diagrams may use simple shape-based indicators inside drawn objects \u2014 see below.)\n- Sentence case on all labels.\n\n**Font size calibration for diagram text labels** - Here's csv table to give you better sense of the Anthropic Sans font rendering width:\n```csv\ntext, chars length, font-weight, font-size, rendered width\nAuthentication Service, chars: 22, font-weight: 500, font-size: 14px, width: 167px\nBackground Job Processor, chars: 24, font-weight: 500, font-size: 14px, width: 201px\nDetects and validates incoming tokens, chars: 37, font-weight: 400, font-size: 14px, width: 279px\nforwards request to, chars: 19, font-weight: 400, font-size: 12px, width: 123px\n\u30c7\u30fc\u30bf\u30d9\u30fc\u30b9\u30b5\u30fc\u30d0\u30fc\u63a5\u7d9a, chars: 12, font-weight: 400, font-size: 14px, width: 181px\n```\n\nBefore placing text in a box, check: does (text width + 2\u00d7padding) fit the container?\n\n**SVG `<text>` never auto-wraps.** Every line break needs an explicit `<tspan x=\"...\" dy=\"1.2em\">`. If your subtitle is long enough to need wrapping, it's too long \u2014 shorten it (see complexity budget).\n\n**Example check**: You want to put \"Glucose (C\u2086H\u2081\u2082O\u2086)\" in a rounded rect. The text is 20 characters at 14px \u2248 180px wide. Add 2\u00d724px padding = 228px minimum box width. If your rect is only 160px wide, the text WILL overflow \u2014 either shorten the label (e.g. just \"Glucose\") or widen the box. Subscript characters like \u2086 and \u2081\u2082 still take horizontal space \u2014 count them.\n\n**Pre-built classes** (already loaded in SVG widget):\n- `class=\"t\"` = sans 14px primary, `class=\"ts\"` = sans 12px secondary, `class=\"th\"` = sans 14px medium (500)\n- `class=\"box\"` = neutral rect (bg-secondary fill, border stroke)\n- `class=\"node\"` = clickable group with hover effect (cursor pointer, slight dim on hover)\n- `class=\"arr\"` = arrow line (1.5px, open chevron head)\n- `class=\"leader\"` = dashed leader line (tertiary stroke, 0.5px, dashed)\n- `class=\"c-{ramp}\"` = colored node (c-blue, c-teal, c-amber, c-green, c-red, c-purple, c-coral, c-pink, c-gray). Apply to `<g>` or shape element (rect/circle/ellipse), NOT to paths. Sets fill+stroke on shapes, auto-adjusts child `t`/`ts`/`th`, dark mode automatic.\n\n**c-{ramp} nesting:** These classes use direct-child selectors (`>`). Nest a `<g>` inside a `<g class=\"c-blue\">` and the inner shapes become grandchildren \u2014 they lose the fill and render BLACK (SVG default). Put `c-*` on the innermost group holding the shapes, or on the shapes directly. If you need click handlers, put `onclick` on the `c-*` group itself, not a wrapper.\n\n- Short aliases: `var(--p)`, `var(--s)`, `var(--t)`, `var(--bg2)`, `var(--b)`\n- Arrow marker: always include this `<defs>` at the start of every SVG:\n  `<defs><marker id=\"arrow\" viewBox=\"0 0 10 10\" refX=\"8\" refY=\"5\" markerWidth=\"6\" markerHeight=\"6\" orient=\"auto-start-reverse\"><path d=\"M2 1L8 5L2 9\" fill=\"none\" stroke=\"context-stroke\" stroke-width=\"1.5\" stroke-linecap=\"round\" stroke-linejoin=\"round\"/></marker></defs>`\n  Then use `marker-end=\"url(#arrow)\"` on lines. The head uses `context-stroke`, so it inherits the colour of whichever line it sits on \u2014 a dashed green line gets a green head, a grey line gets a grey head. Never a colour mismatch. Do not add filters or extra markers to `<defs>`. `<pattern>` fills are allowed when used as a secondary encoding for categorical data \u2014 keep them subtle (thin hatching, sparse dots). Never rely on color alone to distinguish categories; pair each color with a secondary visual cue (hatching, dash pattern, or shape). Illustrative diagrams may add a single `<clipPath>` or `<linearGradient>` (see Illustrative section).\n\n**Minimize standalone labels.** Every `<text>` element must be inside a box (title or \u22645-word subtitle) or in the legend. Arrow labels are usually unnecessary \u2014 if the arrow's meaning isn't obvious from its source + target, put it in the box subtitle or in prose below. Labels floating in space collide with things and are ambiguous.\n\n**Stroke width:** Use 0.5px strokes for diagram borders and edges \u2014 not 1px or 2px. Thin strokes feel more refined.\n\n**Connector paths need `fill=\"none\"`.** SVG defaults to `fill: black` \u2014 a curved connector without `fill=\"none\"` renders as a huge black shape instead of a clean line. Every `<path>` or `<polyline>` used as a connector/arrow MUST have `fill=\"none\"`. Only set fill on shapes meant to be filled (rects, circles, polygons).\n\n**Rect rounding:** `rx=\"4\"` for subtle corners. `rx=\"8\"` max for emphasized rounding. `rx` \u2265 half the height = pill shape \u2014 deliberate only.\n\n**Schematic containers use dashed rects with a label.** Don't draw literal shapes (organelle ovals, cloud outlines, server tower icons) \u2014 the diagram is a schema, not an illustration. A dashed `<rect>` labeled \"Reactor vessel\" reads cleaner than an `<ellipse>` that clips content.\n\n**Lines stop at component edges.** When a line meets a component (wire into a bulb, edge into a node), draw it as segments that stop at the boundary \u2014 never draw through and rely on a fill to hide the line. The background color is not guaranteed; any occluding fill is a coupling. Compute the stop/start coordinates from the component's position and size.\n\n**Physical-color scenes (sky, water, grass, skin, materials):** Use ALL hardcoded hex \u2014 never mix with `c-*` theme classes. The scene should not invert in dark mode. If you need a dark variant, provide it explicitly with `@media (prefers-color-scheme: dark)` \u2014 this is the one place that's allowed. Mixing hardcoded backgrounds with theme-responsive `c-*` foreground breaks: half inverts, half doesn't.\n\n**No rotated text**. `<defs>` may contain the arrow marker, a `<clipPath>`, subtle `<pattern>` fills used as a secondary visual cue alongside color for categorical data, and \u2014 in illustrative diagrams only \u2014 a single `<linearGradient>`. Nothing else: no filters, no extra markers.\n\n\n## Art and illustration\n*\"Draw me a sunset\" / \"Create a geometric pattern\"*\n\nUse SVG. Same technical rules (viewBox, safe area) but the aesthetic is different:\n- Fill the canvas \u2014 art should feel rich, not sparse\n- Bold colors: mix `--color-text-*` categories for variety (info blue, success green, warning amber)\n- Art is the one place custom `<style>` color blocks are fine \u2014 freestyle colors, `prefers-color-scheme` for dark mode variants if you want them\n- Layer overlapping opaque shapes for depth\n- Organic forms with `<path>` curves, `<ellipse>`, `<circle>`\n- Texture via repetition (parallel lines, dots, hatching) not raster effects\n- Geometric patterns with `<g transform=\"rotate()\">` for radial symmetry\n",

landscape', 'white space', 'competitive intelligence',\n];\n\nfunction scoreRelevance(item) {\n  const text = ((item.title || '') + ' ' + (item.summary || '')).toLowerCase();\n  let score = 0;\n  CRITICAL_TERMS.forEach(t => { if (text.includes(t.toLowerCase())) score += 5; });\n  HIGH_IMPACT_TERMS.forEach(t => { if (text.includes(t.toLowerCase())) score += 3; });\n  MEDIUM_IMPACT_TERMS.forEach(t => { if (text.includes(t.toLowerCase())) score += 1; });\n  return score;\n}\n\n// \u2500\u2500 FETCH UTILITIES \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\nfunction fetchUrl(url, timeout = 12000, headers = {}) {\n  return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {\n    const client = url.startsWith('https') ? https : http;\n    const timer = setTimeout(() => reject(new Error('timeout')), timeout);\n    const opts = { headers: { 'User-Agent': 'PatentPreCheck/2.0 legal-research-bot (+https://patentprecheck.com)', ...headers } };\n    client.get(url, opts, (res) => {\n      let data = '';\n      res.on('data', chunk => data += chunk);\n      res.on('end', () => { clearTimeout(timer); resolve({ status: res.statusCode, body: data }); });\n    }).on('error', err => { clearTimeout(timer); reject(err); });\n  });\n}\n\nfunction parseRSS(xml) {\n  const items = [];\n  const itemRegex = /<item>([\\s\\S]*?)<\\/item>/g;\n  let match;\n  while ((match = itemRegex.exec(xml)) !== null) {\n    const item = match[1];\n    const title   = (item.match(/<title><!\\[CDATA\\[(.*?)\\]\\]><\\/title>/) || item.match(/<title>(.*?)<\\/title>/))?.[1]?.trim() || '';\n    const link    = (item.match(/<link>(.*?)<\\/link>/))?.[1]?.trim() || '';\n    const pubDate = (item.match(/<pubDate>(.*?)<\\/pubDate>/))?.[1]?.trim() || '';\n    const desc    = (item.match(/<description><!\\[CDATA\\[(.*?)\\]\\]><\\/description>/) || item.match(/<description>(.*?)<\\/description>/))?.[1]?.replace(/<[^>]+>/g, '')?.slice(0, 600) || '';\n    if (title) items.push({ title, link, date: pubDate, summary: desc });\n  }\n  return items.slice(0, 20);\n}\n\nfunction parseJSON(body, source) {\n  try {\n    const data = JSON.parse(body);\n    const arr = source.jsonPath ? (source.jsonPath.split('.').reduce((o, k) => o?.[k], data) || []) : (Array.isArray(data) ? data : [data]);\n    return arr.slice(0, 20).map(item => ({\n      title:   item[source.titleField] || item.title || item.name || '',\n      link:    (source.urlPrefix || '') + (item[source.urlField] || item.url || item.link || ''),\n      date:    item[source.dateField] || item.date || '',\n      summary: (item.description || item.snippet || item.abstract || '').replace(/<[^>]+>/g,'').slice(0, 500),\n    })).filter(i => i.title);\n  } catch (e) { return []; }\n}\n\n// \u2500\u2500 MAIN FETCH LOOP \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\nasync function fetchAllSources() {\n  const freeSources = SOURCES.filter(s => s.tier <= 2);\n  const premiumSources = SOURCES.filter(s => s.tier >= 3 && ENV[s.requiresKey]);\n\n  console.log(`\\n\u26a1 Patent PreCheck \u2014 Daily Legal Intelligence Fetch v2.0`);\n  console.log(`   ${new Date().toISOString()}`);\n  console.log(`   Free sources:    ${freeSources.length}`);\n  console.log(`   Premium active:  ${premiumSources.length}`);\n  console.log(`   Premium pending: ${SOURCES.filter(s => s.tier >= 3 && !ENV[s.requiresKey]).length} (API keys not configured)`);\n  console.log(`   Monitored cases: ${MONITORED_CASES.length}\\n`);\n\n  const results = [];\n  const errors  = [];\n\n  for (const source of freeSources) {\n    process.stdout.write(`   [T${source.tier}] ${source.name}... `);\n    try {\n      const { status, body } = await fetchUrl(source.url);\n      if (status !== 200) throw new Error(`HTTP ${status}`);\n\n      let items = [];\n      if (source.type === 'rss')  items = parseRSS(body);\n      if (source.type === 'json') items = parseJSON(body, source);\n      if (source.type === 'html') items = [{ title: `${source.name} \u2014 checked`, link: source.url, date: new Date().toISOString(), summary: `Page fetched. ${body.length} chars.` }];\n\n      const scored = items\n        .map(item => ({ ...item, relevance: scoreRelevance(item), source: source.name, sourceId: source.id, tags: source.tags, tier: source.tier, priority: source.priority }))\n        .sort((a, b) => b.relevance - a.relevance);\n\n      const topRelevance = scored[0]?.relevance || 0;\n      const highCount = scored.filter(i => i.relevance >= 5).length;\n      console.log(`\u2713 ${items.length} items | top: ${topRelevance} | critical: ${highCount}`);\n      results.push({ source: source.name, sourceId: source.id, tags: source.tags, tier: source.tier, items: scored, fetchedAt: new Date().toISOString() });\n    } catch (err) {\n      console.log(`\u2717 ${err.message}`);\n      errors.push({ source: source.name, error: err.message });\n    }\n    await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 250));\n  }\n\n  // Log premium sources that need API keys\n  const pendingPremium = SOURCES.filter(s => s.tier >= 3 && !ENV[s.requiresKey]);\n  if (pendingPremium.length) {\n    console.log(`\\n   Premium sources pending API keys:`);\n    pendingPremium.forEach(s => console.log(`   \u2022 ${s.name} (set ${s.requiresKey})`));\n  }\n\n  return { results, errors };\n}\n\n// \u2500\u2500 ALGORITHM SYNTHESIZER \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\nfunction synthesizeAlgorithmUpdate(results) {\n  const allItems = [];\n  results.forEach(r => r.items.forEach(item => { if (item.relevance > 0) allItems.push(item); }));\n  allItems.sort((a, b) => b.relevance - a.relevance);\n  const topItems = allItems.slice(0, 30);\n\n  const categories = {\n    critical_cases: [],   // Alice, Mayo weakening; new CAFC rulings\n    inventorship: [],     // Human conception, Fortress Iron, AI inventorship\n    eligibility: [],      // Section 101, abstract idea\n    guidance: [],         // USPTO/Copyright Office guidance\n    global: [],           // Columbia v. Gen Digital, international\n    legislation: [],      // Rule changes\n    intelligence: [],     // PatSnap, Solve, competitive intel\n    general: [],\n  };\n\n  topItems.forEach(item => {\n    const text = (item.title + ' ' + (item.summary||'')).toLowerCase();\n    if (/columbia|gen digital|overseas|geographic|section 271|global distribution/.test(text)) categories.global.push(item);\n    else if (/fortress iron|inventorship|human conception|inventor|definite.*permanent/.test(text)) categories.inventorship.push(item);\n    else if (/section 101|\u00a7 101|abstract idea|alice|mayo|eligibility/.test(text)) categories.eligibility.push(item);\n    else if (/federal circuit|cafc|supreme court|scotus/.test(text)) categories.critical_cases.push(item);\n    else if (/guidance|guideline|examination|director|memorand/.test(text)) categories.guidance.push(item);\n    else if (/rule change|final rule|proposed rule|regulation/.test(text)) categories.legislation.push(item);\n    else if (/patsnap|solve intel|clarivate|derwent|landscape|white space/.test(text)) categories.intelligence.push(item);\n    else categories.general.push(item);\n  });\n\n  const hasCritical = topItems.some(i => i.relevance >= 10);\n  const hasHigh     = topItems.some(i => i.relevance >= 5);\n  const overallImpact = hasCritical ? 'CRITICAL' : hasHigh ? 'HIGH' : 'MEDIUM';\n\n  return {\n    generatedAt: new Date().toISOString(),\n    overallImpact,\n    sourcesQueried: results.length,\n    premiumSourcesQueried: SOURCES.filter(s => s.tier >= 3 && ENV[s.requiresKey]).length,\n    itemsFound: allItems.length,\n    topItems,\n    categories,\n    monitoredCases: MONITORED_CASES,\n    promptContext: buildPromptContext(topItems, categories),\n  };\n}\n\nfunction buildPromptContext(topItems, categories) {\n  const date = new Date().toDateString();\n  const lines = [\n    `PATENT PRECHECK LEGAL CONTEXT \u2014 Updated ${date}`,\n    `Source tier: 30+ sources including IPWatchdog, Patently-O, Squire Patton Boggs, Federal Circuit via CourtListener, USPTO Federal Register, WIPO, and (when configured) Lexis+ AI, Westlaw Precision, PatSnap, Solve Intelligence, Patent Bots, and Clarivate Derwent.`,\n    `Industry shift: As of 2026, analysis reflects the shift toward agentic AI patent tools that analyze the technical logic of code-related claims, not just keyword search.`,\n    '',\n    '2026 KEY CASE LAW ALERTS:',\n    '\u2022 Columbia University v. Gen Digital (March 2026): Software code reproduced overseas from a U.S. master copy may not qualify for U.S. patent damages. Flag global distribution products.',\n    '\u2022 Fortress Iron: Increased inventorship transparency requirements when AI tools were used. Human conception must be clearly documented.',\n    '\u2022 Director Squires Memoranda (ongoing): Monitor IPWatchdog daily for USPTO Director\\'s latest guidance on AI-assisted inventions and ex parte reexaminations.',\n    '',\n  ];\n\n  if (categories.critical_cases.length) {\n    lines.push('RECENT FEDERAL CIRCUIT / SCOTUS DECISIONS:');\n    categories.critical_cases.slice(0,3).forEach(i => lines.push(`\u2022 ${i.title} (${i.source})`));\n    lines.push('');\n  }\n  if (categories.inventorship.length) {\n    lines.push('RECENT INVENTORSHIP & HUMAN CONCEPTION DEVELOPMENTS:');\n    categories.inventorship.slice(0,3).forEach(i => lines.push(`\u2022 ${i.title}`));\n    lines.push('');\n  }\n  if (categories.eligibility.length) {\n    lines.push('RECENT SECTION 101 / ELIGIBILITY DEVELOPMENTS:');\n    categories.eligibility.slice(0,3).forEach(i => lines.push(`\u2022 ${i.title}`));\n    lines.push('');\n  }\n  if (categories.global.length) {\n    lines.push('GLOBAL / GEOGRAPHIC PATENT DAMAGE DEVELOPMENTS:');\n    categories.global.slice(0,2).forEach(i => lines.push(`\u2022 ${i.title}`));\n    lines.push('');\n  }\n  if (categories.guidance.length) {\n    lines.push('USPTO / COPYRIGHT OFFICE GUIDANCE UPDATES:');\n    categories.guidance.slice(0,3).forEach(i => lines.push(`\u2022 ${i.title}`));\n    lines.push('');\n  }\n\n  lines.push('SCORING GUIDANCE: Apply the following when analyzing AI-assisted work:');\n  lines.push('1. Human conception must be clear and documented. Vague or AI-suggested approaches score lower.');\n  lines.push('2. If the work has global distribution, apply Columbia v. Gen Digital risk flag.');\n  lines.push('3. If AI tools were used without documented human decision-making, apply Fortress Iron penalty.');\n  lines.push('4. Section 101 eligibility: look for specific technical improvements, not just abstract implementation.');\n  lines.push('5. Director Squires\\' 2026 memoranda may affect ex parte reexamination risk \u2014 factor into scoring.');\n\n  return lines.join('\\n');\n}\n\n// \u2500\u2500 USER NOTIFICATION BUILDER \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\nfunction buildUserNotifications(update, previousUpdate) {\n  const notifications = [];\n  if (!previousUpdate) return notifications;\n\n  const prevTitles = new Set((previousUpdate.topItems || []).map(i => i.title));\n  const newCritical = update.topItems.filter(i => i.relevance >= 10 && !prevTitles.has(i.title));\n  const newHigh     = update.topItems.filter(i => i.relevance >= 5  && !prevTitles.has(i.title));\n\n  if (newCritical.length) {\n    notifications.push({\n      type: 'CRITICAL_LAW_CHANGE',\n      priority: 'CRITICAL',\n      audience: 'all_paid_users',\n      subject: `\ud83d\udea8 Critical legal development \u2014 your Patent PreCheck Score may have changed`,\n      body: `We detected ${newCritical.length} critical legal development(s) that may materially affect your patent position:\\n\\n${newCritical.map(i => `\u2022 ${i.title}`).join('\\n')}\\n\\nOur AI Patentability Algorithm has been updated to reflect this. If you have an existing analysis, we recommend re-analyzing your work.\\n\\nRe-analyze now for $19.99 \u2192 patentprecheck.com/reanalyze`,\n      cta: 'Re-analyze \u2014 $19.99',\n      ctaUrl: 'https://patentprecheck.com/reanalyze',\n      items: newCritical,\n    });\n  }\n\n  if (newHigh.length && !newCritical.length) {\n    notifications.push({\n      type: 'LAW_UPDATE',\n      priority: 'HIGH',\n      audience: 'pro_users',\n      subject: `Patent law update \u2014 algorithm refreshed`,\n      body: `We\\'ve updated our AI Patentability Algorithm based on new legal developments:\\n\\n${newHigh.slice(0,3).map(i => `\u2022 ${i.title}`).join('\\n')}\\n\\nYour live Patent PreCheck Score will automatically reflect these changes.`,\n      items: newHigh,\n    });\n  }\n\n  // Always check monitored cases\n  const columbiaHit = update.topItems.find(i => /columbia.*gen digital/i.test(i.title + i.summary));\n  if (columbiaHit && !prevTitles.has(columbiaHit.title)) {\n    notifications.push({\n      type: 'MONITORED_CASE_UPDATE',\n      priority: 'CRITICAL',\n      audience: 'all_users_with_global_products',\n      subject: `\u26a0\ufe0f Columbia v. Gen Digital update \u2014 check your global distribution risk`,\n      body: `New development in Columbia University v. Gen Digital:\\n\\n${columbiaHit.title}\\n\\nThis case directly affects whether overseas software reproduction qualifies for U.S. patent damages. If your product has international distribution, this may affect your scoring.`,\n      items: [columbiaHit],\n    });\n  }\n\n  return notifications;\n}\n\n// \u2500\u2500 MAIN \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\nasync function runDailyUpdate() {\n  const OUTPUT_DIR  = path.join(__dirname, 'legal_data');\n  const TODAY       = new Date().toISOString().slice(0,10);\n  const TODAY_FILE  = path.join(OUTPUT_DIR, `legal_${TODAY}.json`);\n  const LATEST_FILE = path.join(OUTPUT_DIR, 'legal_latest.json');\n  const PREV_FILE   = path.join(OUTPUT_DIR, 'legal_previous.json');\n  const NOTIFY_FILE = path.join(OUTPUT_DIR, 'notifications_pending.json');\n\n  if (!fs.existsSync(OUTPUT_DIR)) fs.mkdirSync(OUTPUT_DIR, { recursive: true });\n\n  let previousUpdate = null;\n  if (fs.existsSync(LATEST_FILE)) {\n    try { previousUpdate = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(LATEST_FILE, 'utf8')); } catch(e) {}\n  }\n\n  const { results, errors } = await fetchAllSources();\n  const update = synthesizeAlgorithmUpdate(results);\n  const notifications = buildUserNotifications(update, previousUpdate);\n\n  // Summary\n  console.log('\\n\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500');\n        ${errors.length}`);\n  console.log(`   Relevant items found:  ${update.itemsFound}`);\n  console.log(`   Overall impact:        ${update.overallImpact}`);\n  console.log(`   Notifications:         ${notifications.length}`);\n  console.log('\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\\n');\n\n  if (update.overallImpact !== 'MEDIUM' && update.topItems.length) {\n    console.log('\ud83d\udd34 TOP ITEMS THIS RUN:');\n    update.topItems.slice(0,5).forEach(i => console.log(`   [${i.relevance}] ${i.title.slice(0,80)}`));\n    console.log('');\n  }\n\n  // Save\n  if (previousUpdate) fs.writeFileSync(PREV_FILE, JSON.stringify(previousUpdate, null, 2));\n  fs.writeFileSync(TODAY_FILE, JSON.stringify({ update, notifications, rawResults: results, errors }, null, 2));\n  fs.writeFileSync(LATEST_FILE, JSON.stringify(update, null, 2));\n  if (notifications.length) {\n    fs.writeFileSync(NOTIFY_FILE, JSON.stringify(notifications, null, 2));\n    console.log(`\ud83d\udcec ${notifications.length} notification(s) written to ${NOTIFY_FILE}`);\n  }\n\n \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\nfunction loadTodaysLegalContext() {\n  const LATEST = path.join(__dirname, 'legal_data', 'legal_latest.json');\n  if (!fs.existsSync(LATEST)) return '';\n  try {\n    const data = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(LATEST, 'utf8'));\n    return data.promptContext || '';\n  } catch (e) { return ''; }\n}\n\n// \u2500\u2500 SOURCE MANIFEST (for site display) getSourceManifest() {\n  return {\n    total: SOURCES.length,\n    free: SOURCES.filter(s => s.tier <= 2).length,\n    premium: SOURCES.filter(s => s.tier >= 3).length,\n    tiers: {\n      1: { label: 'Government & Courts', count: SOURCES.filter(s=>s.tier===1).length, note: 'USPTO, Copyright Office, Federal Circuit, WIPO' },\n      2: { label: 'Legal Blogs & Trackers', count: SOURCES.filter(s=>s.tier===3).length, note: 'Lexis+ AI, Westlaw Precision with KeyCite Overruling Risk' },\n      4: { label: 'AI Patent Intelligence', count: SOURCES.filter(s=>s.tier===4).length, note: 'PatSnap, Solve Intelligence, Patent Bots, Clarivate Derwent' },\n    },\n    monitoredCases: MONITORED_CASES.map(c => ({ id: c.id, name: c.name, impact: c.impact, summary: c.summary })),\n  };\n}\n\nmodule.exports = { runDailyUpdate, loadTodaysLegalContext, getSourceManifest, SOURCES, MONITORED_CASES };\n\nif (require.main === module) {\n  runDailyUpdate().then(() => Any Federal Circuit ruling that weakens Alice immediately triggers an algorithm update and user notifications.',\\n    algorithm_impact: 'If Alice is weakened, software patent eligibility scores increase across the board.',\\n    westlaw_keycite: '573 U.S. 208 (2014)',\\n    tags: ['alice', 'section101', 'foundational', 'monitored'],\\n  },\\n];\\n\\n// \\u2500\\u2500 RELEVANCE SCORING \\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\nconst CRITICAL_TERMS = [\\n  // Foundational cases\\n  'section 101', '\\u00a7 101', 'alice', 'mayo', 'bilski', 'myriad', 'enfish', 'berkheimer',\\n  // 2026 key cases\\n  'columbia university gen digital', 'fortress iron', 'director squires',\\n  // AI inventorship\\n  'ai-assisted invention', 'ai assisted inventor', 'human inventorship', 'human conception',\\n  'definite and permanent idea', 'inventorship transparency',\\n  // AI & patent eligibility\\n  'artificial intelligence patent', 'machine learning patent', 'algorithm patent',\\n  'software patent eligibility', 'abstract idea', 'practical application',\\n  // Agentic & 2026 concepts\\n  'agentic ai', 'human-in-the-loop', 'ai generated code patent',\\n  // Geographic\\n  'overseas software damages', 'global distribution patent', 'section 271',\\n];\\n\\nconst HIGH_IMPACT_TERMS = [\\n  'patent eligibility', 'patentability', 'subject matter eligibility',\\n  'federal circuit', 'cafc', 'supreme court',\\n  'inventorship', 'conception', 'reduction to practice',\\n  'human inventor', 'ai inventor', 'sole inventor',\\n  'guidance update', 'examination guidelines', 'memo', 'memoranda',\\n  'rule change', 'final rule', 'proposed rule',\\n  'overruling', 'distinguish', 'limiting',\\n  'copyright registration', 'authorship', 'work for hire',\\n  'provisional patent', 'utility patent',\\n  'ipwatchdog', 'patently-o', 'squire patton',\\n  'columbia university', 'gen digital',\\n];\\n\\nconst MEDIUM_IMPACT_TERMS = [\\n  'novelty', 'obviousness', 'prior art',\\n  '\\u00a7 102', '\\u00a7 103', '\\u00a7 112', 'written description',\\n  'claim construction', 'claim drafting',\\n  'uspto', 'copyright office', 'ip policy',\\n  'wipo', 'epo', 'international', 'uk ai copyright',\\n  'art unit', 'examiner', 'prosecution history',\\n  'patent landscape', 'white space', 'competitive intelligence',\\n];\\n\\nfunction scoreRelevance(item) {\\n  const text = ((item.title || '') + ' ' + (item.summary || '')).toLowerCase();\\n  let score = 0;\\n  CRITICAL_TERMS.forEach(t => { if (text.includes(t.toLowerCase())) score += 5; });\\n  HIGH_IMPACT_TERMS.forEach(t => { if (text.includes(t.toLowerCase())) score += 3; });\\n  MEDIUM_IMPACT_TERMS.forEach(t => { if (text.includes(t.toLowerCase())) score += 1; });\\n  return score;\\n}\\n\\n// \\u2500\\u2500 FETCH UTILITIES \\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\nfunction fetchUrl(url, timeout = 12000, headers = {}) {\\n  return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {\\n    const client = url.startsWith('https') ? https : http;\\n    const timer = setTimeout(() => reject(new Error('timeout')), timeout);\\n    const opts = { headers: { 'User-Agent': 'PatentPreCheck/2.0 legal-research-bot (+https://patentprecheck.com)', ...headers } };\\n    client.get(url, opts, (res) => {\\n      let data = '';\\n      res.on('data', chunk => data += chunk);\\n      res.on('end', () => { clearTimeout(timer); resolve({ status: res.statusCode, body: data }); });\\n    }).on('error', err => { clearTimeout(timer); reject(err); });\\n  });\\n}\\n\\nfunction parseRSS(xml) {\\n  const items = [];\\n  const itemRegex = /<item>([\\\\s\\\\S]*?)<\\\\/item>/g;\\n  let match;\\n  while ((match = itemRegex.exec(xml)) !== null) {\\n    const item = match[1];\\n    const title   = (item.match(/<title><!\\\\[CDATA\\\\[(.*?)\\\\]\\\\]><\\\\/title>/) || item.match(/<title>(.*?)<\\\\/title>/))?.[1]?.trim() || '';\\n    const link    = (item.match(/<link>(.*?)<\\\\/link>/))?.[1]?.trim() || '';\\n    const pubDate = (item.match(/<pubDate>(.*?)<\\\\/pubDate>/))?.[1]?.trim() || '';\\n    const desc    = (item.match(/<description><!\\\\[CDATA\\\\[(.*?)\\\\]\\\\]><\\\\/description>/) || item.match(/<description>(.*?)<\\\\/description>/))?.[1]?.replace(/<[^>]+>/g, '')?.slice(0, 600) || '';\\n    if (title) items.push({ title, link, date: pubDate, summary: desc });\\n  }\\n  return items.slice(0, 20);\\n}\\n\\nfunction parseJSON(body, source) {\\n  try {\\n    const data = JSON.parse(body);\\n    const arr = source.jsonPath ? (source.jsonPath.split('.').reduce((o, k) => o?.[k], data) || []) : (Array.isArray(data) ? data : [data]);\\n    return arr.slice(0, 20).map(item => ({\\n      title:   item[source.titleField] || item.title || item.name || '',\\n      link:    (source.urlPrefix || '') + (item[source.urlField] || item.url || item.link || ''),\\n      date:    item[source.dateField] || item.date || '',\\n      summary: (item.description || item.snippet || item.abstract || '').replace(/<[^>]+>/g,'').slice(0, 500),\\n    })).filter(i => i.title);\\n  } catch (e) { return []; }\\n}\\n\\n// \\u2500\\u2500 MAIN FETCH LOOP \\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\nasync function fetchAllSources() {\\n  const freeSources = SOURCES.filter(s => s.tier <= 2);\\n  const premiumSources = SOURCES.filter(s => s.tier >= 3 && ENV[s.requiresKey]);\\n\\n  console.log(`\\\\n\\u26a1 Patent PreCheck \\u2014 Daily Legal Intelligence Fetch v2.0`);\\n  console.log(`   ${new Date().toISOString()}`);\\n  console.log(`   Free sources:    ${freeSources.length}`);\\n  console.log(`   Premium active:  ${premiumSources.length}`);\\n  console.log(`   Premium pending: ${SOURCES.filter(s => s.tier >= 3 && !ENV[s.requiresKey]).length} (API keys not configured)`);\\n  console.log(`   Monitored cases: ${MONITORED_CASES.length}\\\\n`);\\n\\n  const results = [];\\n  const errors  = [];\\n\\n  for (const source of freeSources) {\\n    process.stdout.write(`   [T${source.tier}] ${source.name}... `);\\n    try {\\n      const { status, body } = await fetchUrl(source.url);\\n      if (status !== 200) throw new Error(`HTTP ${status}`);\\n\\n      let items = [];\\n      if (source.type === 'rss')  items = parseRSS(body);\\n      if (source.type === 'json') items = parseJSON(body, source);\\n      if (source.type === 'html') items = [{ title: `${source.name} \\u2014 checked`, link: source.url, date: new Date().toISOString(), summary: `Page fetched. ${body.length} chars.` }];\\n\\n      const scored = items\\n        .map(item => ({ ...item, relevance: scoreRelevance(item), source: source.name, sourceId: source.id, tags: source.tags, tier: source.tier, priority: source.priority }))\\n        .sort((a, b) => b.relevance - a.relevance);\\n\\n      const topRelevance = scored[0]?.relevance || 0;\\n      const highCount = scored.filter(i => i.relevance >= 5).length;\\n      console.log(`\\u2713 ${items.length} items | top: ${topRelevance} | critical: ${highCount}`);\\n      results.push({ source: source.name, sourceId: source.id, tags: source.tags, tier: source.tier, items: scored, fetchedAt: new Date().toISOString() });\\n    } catch (err) {\\n      console.log(`\\u2717 ${err.message}`);\\n      errors.push({ source: source.name, error: err.message });\\n    }\\n    await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 250));\\n  }\\n\\n  // Log premium sources that need API keys\\n  const pendingPremium = SOURCES.filter(s => s.tier >= 3 && !ENV[s.requiresKey]);\\n  if (pendingPremium.length) {\\n    console.log(`\\\\n   Premium sources pending API keys:`);\\n    pendingPremium.forEach(s => console.log(`   \\u2022 ${s.name} (set ${s.requiresKey})`));\\n  }\\n\\n  return { results, errors };\\n}\\n\\n// \\u2500\\u2500 ALGORITHM SYNTHESIZER \\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\nfunction synthesizeAlgorithmUpdate(results) {\\n  const allItems = [];\\n  results.forEach(r => r.items.forEach(item => { if (item.relevance > 0) allItems.push(item); }));\\n  allItems.sort((a, b) => b.relevance - a.relevance);\\n  const topItems = allItems.slice(0, 30);\\n\\n  const categories = {\\n    critical_cases: [],   // Alice, Mayo weakening; new CAFC rulings\\n    inventorship: [],     // Human conception, Fortress Iron, AI inventorship\\n    eligibility: [],      // Section 101, abstract idea\\n    guidance: [],         // USPTO/Copyright Office guidance\\n    global: [],           // Columbia v. Gen Digital, international\\n    legislation: [],      // Rule changes\\n    intelligence: [],     // PatSnap, Solve, competitive intel\\n    general: [],\\n  };\\n\\n  topItems.forEach(item => {\\n    const text = (item.title + ' ' + (item.summary||'')).toLowerCase();\\n    if (/columbia|gen digital|overseas|geographic|section 271|global distribution/.test(text)) categories.global.push(item);\\n    else if (/fortress iron|inventorship|human conception|inventor|definite.*permanent/.test(text)) categories.inventorship.push(item);\\n    else if (/section 101|\\u00a7 101|abstract idea|alice|mayo|eligibility/.test(text)) categories.eligibility.push(item);\\n    else if (/federal circuit|cafc|supreme court|scotus/.test(text)) categories.critical_cases.push(item);\\n    else if (/guidance|guideline|examination|director|memorand/.test(text)) categories.guidance.push(item);\\n    else if (/rule change|final rule|proposed rule|regulation/.test(text)) categories.legislation.push(item);\\n    else if (/patsnap|solve intel|clarivate|derwent|landscape|white space/.test(text)) categories.intelligence.push(item);\\n    else categories.general.push(item);\\n  });\\n\\n  const hasCritical = topItems.some(i => i.relevance >= 10);\\n  const hasHigh     = topItems.some(i => i.relevance >= 5);\\n  const overallImpact = hasCritical ? 'CRITICAL' : hasHigh ? 'HIGH' : 'MEDIUM';\\n\\n  return {\\n    generatedAt: new Date().toISOString(),\\n    overallImpact,\\n    sourcesQueried: results.length,\\n    premiumSourcesQueried: SOURCES.filter(s => s.tier >= 3 && ENV[s.requiresKey]).length,\\n    itemsFound: allItems.length,\\n    topItems,\\n    categories,\\n    monitoredCases: MONITORED_CASES,\\n    promptContext: buildPromptContext(topItems, categories),\\n  };\\n}\\n\\nfunction buildPromptContext(topItems, categories) {\\n  const date = new Date().toDateString();\\n  const lines = [\\n    `PATENT PRECHECK LEGAL CONTEXT \\u2014 Updated ${date}`,\\n    `Source tier: 30+ sources including IPWatchdog, Patently-O, Squire Patton Boggs, Federal Circuit via CourtListener, USPTO Federal Register, WIPO, and (when configured) Lexis+ AI, Westlaw Precision, PatSnap, Solve Intelligence, Patent Bots, and Clarivate Derwent.`,\\n    `Industry shift: As of 2026, analysis reflects the shift toward agentic AI patent tools that analyze the technical logic of code-related claims, not just keyword search.`,\\n    '',\\n    '2026 KEY CASE LAW ALERTS:',\\n    '\\u2022 Columbia University v. Gen Digital (March 2026): Software code reproduced overseas from a U.S. master copy may not qualify for U.S. patent damages. Flag global distribution products.',\\n    '\\u2022 Fortress Iron: Increased inventorship transparency requirements when AI tools were used. Human conception must be clearly documented.',\\n    '\\u2022 Director Squires Memoranda (ongoing): Monitor IPWatchdog daily for USPTO Director\\\\'s latest guidance on AI-assisted inventions and ex parte reexaminations.',\\n    '',\\n  ];\\n\\n  if (categories.critical_cases.length) {\\n    lines.push('RECENT FEDERAL CIRCUIT / SCOTUS DECISIONS:');\\n    categories.critical_cases.slice(0,3).forEach(i => lines.push(`\\u2022 ${i.title} (${i.source})`));\\n    lines.push('');\\n  }\\n  if (categories.inventorship.length) {\\n    lines.push('RECENT INVENTORSHIP & HUMAN CONCEPTION DEVELOPMENTS:');\\n    categories.inventorship.slice(0,3).forEach(i => lines.push(`\\u2022 ${i.title}`));\\n    lines.push('');\\n  }\\n  if (categories.eligibility.length) {\\n    lines.push('RECENT SECTION 101 / ELIGIBILITY DEVELOPMENTS:');\\n    categories.eligibility.slice(0,3).forEach(i => lines.push(`\\u2022 ${i.title}`));\\n    lines.push('');\\n  }\\n  if (categories.global.length) {\\n    lines.push('GLOBAL / GEOGRAPHIC PATENT DAMAGE DEVELOPMENTS:');\\n    categories.global.slice(0,2).forEach(i => lines.push(`\\u2022 ${i.title}`));\\n    lines.push('');\\n  }\\n  if (categories.guidance.length) {\\n    lines.push('USPTO / COPYRIGHT OFFICE GUIDANCE UPDATES:');\\n    categories.guidance.slice(0,3).forEach(i => lines.push(`\\u2022 ${i.title}`));\\n    lines.push('');\\n  }\\n\\n  lines.push('SCORING GUIDANCE: Apply the following when analyzing AI-assisted work:');\\n  lines.push('1. Human conception must be clear and documented. Vague or AI-suggested approaches score lower.');\\n  lines.push('2. If the work has global distribution, apply Columbia v. Gen Digital risk flag.');\\n  lines.push('3. If AI tools were used without documented human decision-making, apply Fortress Iron penalty.');\\n  lines.push('4. Section 101 eligibility: look for specific technical improvements, not just abstract implementation.');\\n  lines.push('5. Director Squires\\\\' 2026 memoranda may affect ex parte reexamination risk \\u2014 factor into scoring.');\\n\\n  return lines.join('\\\\n');\\n}\\n\\n// \\u2500\\u2500 USER NOTIFICATION BUILDER \\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\nfunction buildUserNotifications(update, previousUpdate) {\\n  const notifications = [];\\n  if (!previousUpdate) return notifications;\\n\\n  const prevTitles = new Set((previousUpdate.topItems || []).map(i => i.title));\\n  const newCritical = update.topItems.filter(i => i.relevance >= 10 && !prevTitles.has(i.title));\\n  const newHigh     = update.topItems.filter(i => i.relevance >= 5  && !prevTitles.has(i.title));\\n\\n  if (newCritical.length) {\\n    notifications.push({\\n      type: 'CRITICAL_LAW_CHANGE',\\n      priority: 'CRITICAL',\\n      audience: 'all_paid_users',\\n      subject: `\\ud83d\\udea8 Critical legal development \\u2014 your Patent PreCheck Score may have changed`,\\n      body: `We detected ${newCritical.length} critical legal development(s) that may materially affect your patent position:\\\\n\\\\n${newCritical.map(i => `\\u2022 ${i.title}`).join('\\\\n')}\\\\n\\\\nOur AI Patentability Algorithm has been updated to reflect this. postJSON(url, payload, extraHeaders = {}) {\n  return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {\n    const body   = JSON.stringify(payload);\n    const parsed = new URL(url);\n    const opts   = {\n      hostname: parsed.hostname,\n      path:     parsed.pathname + parsed.search,\n      method:   'POST',\n      headers:  { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', 'Content-Length': Buffer.byteLength(body), ...extraHeaders },\n    };\n    const client = url.startsWith('https') ? https : http;\n    const req = client.request(opts, (res) => {\n      let data = '';\n      res.on('data', c => data += c);\n      res.on('end', () => {\n        if (res.statusCode >= 200 && res.statusCode < 300) {\n          resolve({ ok: true, status: res.statusCode });\n        } else {\n          reject(new Error(`HTTP ${res.statusCode}: ${data.slice(0,200)}`));\n        }\n      });\n    });\n    req.on('error', reject);\n    req.write(body);\n    req.end();\n  });\n}\n\n// \u2500\u2500 DAILY DIGEST for internal team function sendDailyDigest(update, notifications) {\n  if (!ENV.NOTIFY_EMAIL_TO && !ENV.SLACK_WEBHOOK) return;\n\n  const date     = new Date().toLocaleDateString('en-US', { weekday:'long', year:'numeric', month:'long', day:'numeric' });\n  const topItems = (update.topItems || []).slice(0, 10);\n  const notifSummary = notifications.length\n    ? `${notifications.length} user notification(s) queued \u2014 ${notifications.map(n=>n.type).join(', ')}`\n    : 'No material changes requiring user notifications';\n\n  const digestNotif = {\n    type:     'DAILY_DIGEST',\n    priority: update.overallImpact,\n    subject:  `\ud83d\udccb Patent PreCheck Legal Digest \u2014 ${date}`,\n    audience: 'internal_team',\n    body: `Daily legal intelligence summary:\\n\\n` +\n      `Sources queried: ${update.sourcesQueried}\\n` +\n      `Relevant items: ${update.itemsFound}\\n` +\n      `Overall impact: ${update.overallImpact}\\n` +\n      `User notifications: ${notifSummary}\\n\\n` +\n      `Top items:\\n${topItems.map(i => `[${i.relevance}] ${i.title.slice(0,80)} (${i.source})`).join('\\n')}`,\n    items: topItems,\n    cta:    null,\n    ctaUrl: null,\n  };\n\n  if (ENV.SLACK_WEBHOOK) {\n    try {\n      await sendSlack(digestNotif, update.overallImpact);\n      console.log('   [Slack] Daily digest sent \u2713');\n    } catch (e) {\n      console.log(`   [Slack] Error: ${e.message}`);\n    }\n  }\n\n  if (ENV.SENDGRID_API_KEY && ENV.NOTIFY_EMAIL_TO) {\n    try {\n      await sendEmail(digestNotif, update.overallImpact, ENV.NOTIFY_EMAIL_TO);\n      console.log(`   [Email] Daily digest \u2192 ${ENV.NOTIFY_EMAIL_TO} \u2713`);\n    } catch (e) {\n      console.log(`   [Email] Error: ${e.message}`);\n    }\n  }\n}\n\n// \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\nasync function dispatchNotifications(notifications, update) {\n  console.log(`\\n\ud83d\udcec Dispatching ${notifications.length} notification(s)...`);\n\n  // Internal digest always goes out\n  await ENV.NOTIFY_EMAIL_TO);\n        console.log(`     [Email] \u2713`);\n      } catch (e) {\n        console.log(`     [Email] Error: ${e.message}`);\n      }\n    }\n  }\n}\n\nmodule.exports = { Or, perhaps, does the lack of a human inventor in this case preclude that device from being patented at all? The USPTO guidance makes it clear that while AI-assisted inventions are not \u201ccategorically unpatentable,\u201d AI systems themselves are not individuals and therefore cannot be inventors, legally speaking. Therefore, it follows that at least one human must be named as the inventor of any given claim. \n\n(There are actually some interesting parallels to the infamous \u201cmonkey selfie\u201d case \u2014 where the monkey obviously taking the photo can\u2019t be awarded copyright, because copyrights must be owned by legal persons, and monkeys, though they are many things, are not that.)\n\nMerely recognizing a problem or having a general goal or research plan to pursue does not rise to the level of conception. A natural person who only presents a problem to an AI system may not be a proper inventor or joint inventor of an invention identified from the output of the AI system. However, a significant contribution could be shown by the way the person constructs the prompt in view of a specific problem to elicit a particular solution from the AI system. \u2026A natural person who merely recognizes and appreciates the output of an AI system as an invention, particularly when the properties and utility of the output are apparent to those of ordinary skill, is not necessarily an inventor. \n\nHowever, a person who takes the ou",

'000000', italics: opts.italics ?? false })]\n});\n\nconst h1 = (text) => new Paragraph({\n  heading: HeadingLevel.HEADING_1,\n  spacing: { before: 400, after: 120 },\n  children: [new TextRun({ text, bold: true, color: NAVY, size: 36 })]\n});\n\nconst h2 = (text) => new Paragraph({\n  heading: HeadingLevel.HEADING_2,\n  spacing: { before: 280, after: 100 },\n  children: [new TextRun({ text, bold: true, color: BLUE, size: 28 })]\n});\n\nconst mixedPara = (runs) => new Paragraph({\n  spacing: { before: 80, after: 100 },\n  children: runs\n});\n\nconst run  = (text, opts = {}) => new TextRun({ text, size: opts.size ?? 22, bold: opts.bold ?? false, color: opts.color ?? [new TextRun({ text: label + ': ', bold: true, size: 22, color: NAVY }), new TextRun({ text: rest, size: 22 })]\n    : [new TextRun({ text: label || rest, size: 22 })]\n});\n\nconst divider = () => new Paragraph({\n  spacing: { before: 200, after: 200 },\n  border: { bottom: { style: BorderStyle.SINGLE, size: 6, color: LGRAY } },\n  children: []\n});\n\n// \u2500\u2500 BUILD DOC \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\nconst doc = new Document({\n  creator: 'Kevin J. Gen Digital</h3>\n          <p>March 2026 Federal Circuit decision clarified that software code reproduced overseas from a U.S. master copy may not qualify for U.S. patent damages under \u00a7271(f). Gen Digital</h3>\\n          <p>March 2026 Federal Circuit decision clarified that software code reproduced overseas from a U.S. master copy may not qualify for U.S. patent damages under \\u00a7271(f). Any software product with global distribution now carries additional geographic patent risk.</p>\\n          <p style=\\\"margin-top:8px; font-size:13px; color:var(--green); font-weight:500;\\\">\\u2192 Algorithm updated same day. Vague or undocumented human contributions now carry higher invalidation risk.</p>\\n          <p style=\\\"margin-top:8px; font-size:13px; color:var(--green); font-weight:500;\\\">\\u2192 Documentation scoring weight increased. Each memo affects how examiners evaluate human conception evidence.</p>\\n          <p style=\\\"margin-top:8px; font-size:13px; color:var(--green); font-weight:500;\\\">\\u2192 Each new memorandum incorporated within 24 hours. This means creating files like `logo-nav.svg` for the horizontal version with white text and green mark elements that work on dark backgrounds.\n\nBut wait\u2014SVG files rendered as images won't have access to the page's CSS fonts, so I'd need to either import Google Fonts inside the SVG itself or rely on system fonts like Georgia. The more reliable solution is to embed the SVG directly in the HTML as inline code, which lets it inherit the page's loaded Playfair Display font and gives me better control overall.\n\nNow I'm ready to write the update script. NAV BACKGROUND \u2192 light grey on all pages \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n    # Replace any dark navy nav background\n    html = re.sub(\n        r'(nav\\s*\\{[^}]*?background\\s*:)[^;]+;',\n        r'\\1 rgba(248,250,255,0.97);',\n        html\n    )\n    # Also handle nav { ... } blocks that set background via variable\n    html = html.replace('background:var(--navy);padding:0', \n                        'background:rgba(248,250,255,0.97);padding:0')\n    # Add box-shadow for depth on light nav (replace existing or add)\n    html = re.sub(\n        r'(nav\\s*\\{[^}]*?)(border-bottom[^;]+;)',\n        r'\\1border-bottom:1px solid #E2E8F4;',\n        html\n    )\n\n    # \u2500\u2500 2. NAV LINK COLORS \u2192 dark on light nav \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n    # Ensure nav-link, nav-links-a are dark colored\n    html = re.sub(\n        r'(\\.nav-link\\s*\\{[^}]*?color\\s*:)\\s*[^;]+;',\n        r'\\1 #0C2340;',\n        html\n    )\n    html = re.sub(\n        r'(\\.nav-a\\s*\\{[^}]*?color\\s*:)\\s*[^;]+;',\n        r'\\1 #0C2340;',\n        html\n    )\n    # nav-links a color\n    html = re.sub(\n        r'(\\.nav-links\\s+a\\s*\\{[^}]*?color\\s*:)\\s*[^;]+;',\n        r'\\1 #0C2340;',\n        html\n    )\n    # Fix any explicit white nav link colors\n    html = re.sub(\n        r'(\\.nav-links?\\s*a\\s*\\{[^\\}]*?)color\\s*:\\s*rgba\\(255,255,255[^)]*\\)',\n        r'\\1color:#4A5568',\n        html\n    )\n\n    # \u2500\u2500 3. REPLACE NAV LOGO SVG (update to dark text version) \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n    html = re.sub(\n        r'(<a[^>]+(?:nav-logo|logo)[^>]*>)\\s*<svg[^>]*height=\"36\"[^>]*>.*?</svg>',\n        r'\\1' + LOGO_LIGHT,\n        html, flags=re.DOTALL\n    )\n\n    # \u2500\u2500 4. CTA BUTTON SPACING \u2192 more gap from nav links \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n    # Move nav-cta outside nav-links div if it's currently inside\n    # Pattern: nav-cta is the last child of nav-links\n    html = re.sub(\n        r'(<a[^>]+nav-cta[^>]*>(?:Add to Chrome[^<]*|Check my work[^<]*)</a>)\\s*(</div>\\s*</nav>)',\n        lambda m: m.group(2).replace('</div>', '') + f'  {m.group(1)}\\n</nav>',\n        html, flags=re.DOTALL\n    )\n    # Simpler: just add margin-left to .nav-cta CSS\n    html = re.sub(\n        r'(\\.nav-cta\\s*\\{)',\n        r'\\1 margin-left:24px;',\n        html\n    )\n    # For the logo on analyze.html which uses class=\"logo\" not \"nav-logo\"\n    html = re.sub(\n        r'(<a[^>]+class=\"logo\"[^>]*>)\\s*<svg[^>]*height=\"36\"[^>]*>.*?</svg>',\n        r'\\1' + LOGO_LIGHT,\n        html, flags=re.DOTALL\n    )\n\n    # \u2500\u2500 5. REFRESH FOOTER LOGO (remove old, add fresh) \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n    html = re.sub(r'\\s*<div style=\"text-align:center;padding:0 0 28px\">.*?</div>\\s*', \n                  '\\n  ', html, flags=re.DOTALL)\n    html = re.sub(r'(<footer[^>]*>)', r'\\1\\n  ' + FOOTER_LOGO_HTML, html, count=1)\n\n    if html != orig:\n        with open(fpath, 'w') as f:\n            f.write(html)\n        print(f'\u2713 {fname}')\n    else:\n        print(f'\u2014 {fname} (no changes)')\n\nprint('\\nPackaging...')\nimport zipfile, shutil\nzip_path = '/home/claude/patentprecheck-site.zip'\nwith zipfile.ZipFile(zip_path, 'w', zipfile.ZIP_DEFLATED) as zf:\n    for root, dirs, files in os.walk(site_dir):\n        for file in files:\n            fp = os.path.join(root, file)\n            arcname = os.path.relpath(fp, os.path.dirname(site_dir))\n            zf.write(fp, arcname)\nshutil.copy(zip_path, '/mnt/user-data/outputs/patentprecheck-site.zip')\nfor f in ['index.html','analyze.html','legal-intelligence.html']:\n    shutil.copy(f'{site_dir}/{f}', f'/mnt/user-data/outputs/{f}')\nprint(f'Done \u2014 {os.path.getsize(zip_path)//1024}KB')\nPYEOF",

pages:\\n    fpath = os.path.join(site_dir, fname)\\n    if not os.path.exists(fpath): continue\\n    with open(fpath) as f:\\n        html = f.read()\\n    orig = html\\n\\n    # 1. Replace existing logo SVG (any height) with new larger one\\n    html = re.sub(\\n        r'(<a[^>]+(?:nav-logo|class=\\\"logo\\\")[^>]*>)\\\\s*<svg[^>]*height=\\\"[^\\\"]*\\\"[^>]*viewBox=\\\"0 0 \\\\d+ \\\\d+\\\"[^>]*>.*?</svg>',\\n        r'\\\\1' + LOGO_LIGHT_LARGE,\\n        html, flags=re.DOTALL\\n    )\\n\\n    # 2. Ensure CTA button is OUTSIDE nav-links div\\n    # Move any nav-cta that's inside .nav-links to after the closing </div>\\n    def move_cta_out(m):\\n        links_open = m.group(1)\\n        links_content = m.group(2)\\n        links_close = m.group(3)\\n        nav_close = m.group(4)\\n        # Extract cta from links_content\\n        cta_match = re.search(r'\\\\s*<a[^>]+(?:nav-cta|nav-btn)[^>]*>[^<]*(?:Check|Add)[^<]*</a>', links_content)\\n        if cta_match:\\n            cta = cta_match.group(0).strip()\\n            new_links = links_content.replace(cta_match.group(0), '')\\n            return f'{links_open}{new_links}{links_close}\\\\n  {cta}\\\\n{nav_close}'\\n        return m.group(0)\\n    \\n    html = re.sub(\\n        r'(<div class=\\\"nav-(?:links|r)\\\"[^>]*>)(.*?)(</div>)\\\\s*(</nav>)',\\n        move_cta_out, html, flags=re.DOTALL\\n    )\\n\\n    # 4.
